title: Duplicate orders and billing questions
text:
# Duplicate orders and billing questions

If you see more than one charge from Fieldstone Goods on your statement, it may be a pending authorization rather than a duplicate order. Authorization holds appear alongside final charges for a few days before your bank removes them. Any billing question that requires refund review or dispute resolution is handled by a human specialist—Ember, our support agent, cannot process these requests directly.

title: Duplicate orders and billing questions
text:
# Duplicate orders and billing questions

## Understanding authorization holds vs. charges

When you place an order, your bank may show two transactions: a pending authorization and a completed charge. The authorization is a temporary hold that verifies funds are available. It typically drops off your statement within 2–5 business days, depending on your bank. Only one charge will finalize.

If both transactions remain as completed charges after five days, that indicates a potential duplicate order. Check your order confirmation emails—if you received two separate order numbers (format FG-NNNNNN), two orders were placed. If you only have one confirmation email but see two charges, a billing specialist will investigate.

title: Duplicate orders and billing questions
section_heading: What to do if you think you were charged twice
text:
# Duplicate orders and billing questions

## What to do if you think you were charged twice

First, confirm whether both charges have moved from "pending" to "posted" on your statement. If one is still pending, wait three business days for your bank to clear the authorization.

If both charges are posted and you only intended to place one order, gather your order number and the last four digits of the payment method used. Contact Fieldstone customer support for review. A billing specialist will compare the transaction details against our order records and issue a refund if a duplicate charge occurred. See our guide on contacting customer support for the fastest way to reach the team.

Refunds for confirmed duplicate charges are processed to the original payment method within 5–7 business days of verification.

title: Duplicate orders and billing questions
section_heading: Why billing questions require a specialist
text:
# Duplicate orders and billing questions

## Why billing questions require a specialist

Ember, our support agent, can help with order tracking, return initiation, and policy questions. However, billing disputes, duplicate charge reviews, and refund exceptions fall outside what Ember can resolve. These requests require manual review of payment processor records and sometimes coordination with your bank.

When you contact support about a billing issue, your inquiry is routed to a specialist who has access to transaction logs and refund tools. This ensures accurate resolution and protects your payment information.

title: Duplicate orders and billing questions
text:
# Duplicate orders and billing questions

## FAQ

**Can I cancel an order to avoid a duplicate charge?**  
Orders can only be canceled before they ship. If your order has already entered fulfillment, you will need to return the item once it arrives. The standard 30-day return window applies, and refunds land on your original payment method within 5–7 business days of the warehouse receiving your return.

**What if I see a charge but never received a confirmation email?**  
Check your spam folder first. If you still cannot locate a confirmation, contact support with the charge amount, date, and last four digits of your payment method. A specialist will search for the order and resend your confirmation or investigate the charge.

**How long does a billing dispute take to resolve?**  
Most billing reviews are completed within 3–5 business days. Complex cases involving payment processor records may take up to ten business days. You will receive an email update once the specialist completes their review.

title: Finding your order number
text:
# Finding your order number

Your Fieldstone order number is the six-digit code we send after every purchase, formatted FG-NNNNNN. You need this number to track shipments, start a return, or reach our support team. It appears in three emails we send automatically — check your inbox and spam folder for messages from orders@fieldstonegoods.com.

title: Finding your order number
text:
# Finding your order number

## Where your order number appears

**Confirmation email subject line**  
Within minutes of checkout, you receive a confirmation email with a subject like "Your Fieldstone order FG-123456 is confirmed." The number sits right in the subject, making it easy to search.

**Confirmation email body**  
Open that same email and scroll to the top. Just below the "Thank you" heading, you'll see your order number in bold, followed by your order date and total. This is the clearest place to copy the full number.

**Shipping notification email**  
When your order ships, we send a second email with the subject "Your Fieldstone order has shipped." The body includes your order number, tracking link, and estimated delivery window. If you saved the shipping email but deleted the confirmation, this is a reliable backup.

title: Finding your order number
section_heading: If you cannot locate the email
text:
# Finding your order number

## If you cannot locate the email

Start by searching your inbox for "fieldstonegoods.com" or "FG-" to surface any message containing the number. Check your spam and promotions folders — confirmation emails sometimes land there.

If the email is truly missing, contact our support team at help@fieldstonegoods.com. Include the email address you used at checkout and an approximate order date. A specialist will locate your order and reply with your number, usually within one business day. See our guide on How Fieldstone order numbers work for details on the format and what each part means.

title: Finding your order number
section_heading: Why you need your order number
text:
# Finding your order number

## Why you need your order number

Fieldstone does not use customer accounts in the traditional sense. Instead, we verify your identity with your email address and order number each time you interact with us. When you request a return, check order status, or ask a question, you'll enter both pieces of information. Keep your confirmation email in a safe folder so you can reference it quickly.

title: Finding your order number
text:
# Finding your order number

## FAQ

**Can I use my shipping tracking number instead of my order number?**  
No. The tracking number identifies the carrier's shipment, but our systems require the Fieldstone order number (FG-NNNNNN) to pull up your purchase details, process returns, or apply adjustments.

**What if I placed multiple orders and lost track of which number belongs to which purchase?**  
Each confirmation email lists the items in that specific order. Search your inbox for the product name or SKU to narrow down the right message. If you're still unsure, email help@fieldstonegoods.com with the items you're asking about and we'll match them to the correct order.

**I found an old order number — does it expire?**  
Order numbers remain valid indefinitely. Even for purchases outside the 30-day return window, the number still works for order history inquiries and warranty questions.

title: How Fieldstone order numbers work
text:
# How Fieldstone order numbers work

Every Fieldstone order gets a unique identifier in the format FG-NNNNNN, where the six digits make your order trackable. This number is the primary way our support team verifies your identity and pulls up your order details. You'll need it for returns, tracking questions, and any other help with your purchase.

Because Fieldstone doesn't use traditional user accounts, your order number combined with the email address used at checkout is how you confirm your identity. Think of the order number as your receipt — keep it handy, especially if you're reaching out for support.

title: How Fieldstone order numbers work
text:
# How Fieldstone order numbers work

## What the format means

Fieldstone order numbers always start with "FG-" followed by six digits. For example: FG-123456. This format makes it easy to distinguish order numbers from other reference codes like tracking numbers or SKUs. You'll see it prominently in your order confirmation email, shipping notifications, and packing slip.

The six-digit sequence is unique to your order. No two orders share the same number, so providing this identifier ensures support is looking at the right purchase.

title: How Fieldstone order numbers work
section_heading: Where to find your order number
text:
# How Fieldstone order numbers work

## Where to find your order number

Your order number appears in the subject line and body of your order confirmation email, which is sent immediately after checkout. It's also printed at the top of your packing slip inside the shipping box. See our guide on finding your order number for screenshots and additional locations.

If you've deleted your confirmation email, check your shipping notification — the order number appears there as well. The tracking page linked in that email also displays the order number once you arrive.

title: How Fieldstone order numbers work
text:
# How Fieldstone order numbers work

## Why support needs this number

When you contact Fieldstone with a question about an order, the support agent (or our assistant Ember) will ask for your order number and the email address used at checkout. This two-part verification lets us locate your order and confirm it's yours before discussing details or processing a return.

Without the order number, we cannot retrieve your order information. Fieldstone doesn't store purchase history under an account dashboard, so the order number is the only direct path to your order record.

title: How Fieldstone order numbers work
text:
# How Fieldstone order numbers work

## FAQ

**What if I have multiple orders?** Each order has its own unique FG-NNNNNN number, even if placed on the same day with the same email address. You'll receive separate confirmation emails for each order, and each will include its distinct order number.

**Can I look up my order with just my email address?** No. For security and accuracy, we require both the order number and the email address. The order number ensures we're reviewing the specific purchase you're asking about, especially if you've placed multiple orders.

**Is the order number the same as a tracking number?** No. Your tracking number comes from the shipping carrier (like USPS or FedEx) and appears in your shipping notification. The order number is Fieldstone's internal identifier. Both are useful, but support requests should always include the FG-NNNNNN order number.

title: Order history without an account
text:
# Order history without an account

Fieldstone does not use traditional customer accounts. Instead, you access your orders using your email address and order number. Every confirmation email contains the information you need to look up your order status, track shipments, and start returns.

This guide explains how to retrieve past orders, what information you'll need, and how the system keeps your orders organized.

title: Order history without an account
section_heading: How to find a specific order
text:
# Order history without an account

## How to find a specific order

Each order confirmation email includes your unique order number in the format FG-NNNNNN (for example, FG-123456). To look up an order:

1. Open the confirmation email for that order
2. Note the order number in the subject line or email body
3. Visit the order lookup page and enter your email address and order number
4. View your order details, tracking information, and available actions

If you cannot locate the confirmation email, check your spam folder or search your inbox for "Fieldstone order confirmation." See our guide on Finding your order number for additional tips.

title: Order history without an account
text:
# Order history without an account

## Accessing multiple orders

Because Fieldstone does not maintain a dashboard of all your purchases, you'll need to reference each confirmation email individually. If you've placed several orders:

- Keep confirmation emails in a dedicated folder
- Search your inbox by order number (FG-) to see all Fieldstone purchases
- Use the same email address for future orders to keep everything in one inbox

Each order stands alone. You cannot view a combined history, but your confirmation emails serve as a complete record of everything you've ordered.

title: Order history without an account
section_heading: What you can do with order lookup
text:
# Order history without an account

## What you can do with order lookup

Once you enter your email and order number, you can:

- Check the current shipping status and carrier tracking link
- Review which items are in the order and their prices
- Start a return for eligible items within the 30-day window
- Confirm your delivery address

You do not need to create an account to perform any of these actions. The lookup system handles returns, exchanges, and order updates.

title: Order history without an account
text:
# Order history without an account

## Account features in development

Fieldstone is building optional account features for customers who want a centralized view of their order history. These features are not yet available, but when they launch, you'll be able to:

- See all past orders in one place
- Save shipping addresses for faster checkout
- Opt in to reorder reminders for frequently purchased items

For now, your confirmation emails remain the primary way to track and manage orders. They contain everything you need to get help, check status, or initiate a return.

title: Order history without an account
text:
# Order history without an account

## FAQ

**What if I used different email addresses for different orders?**  
Each order is tied to the email address you provided at checkout. You'll need to look up orders separately using the matching email and order number for each one.

**Can I update my email address after placing an order?**  
Email addresses cannot be changed after an order is placed, since the confirmation email serves as your access credential. Future orders can use a different address if needed.

**Do I need to keep the confirmation email forever?**  
Keep confirmation emails at least until your return window closes (30 days from delivery). After that, they're useful for warranty reference or reordering the same items.

title: Updating the email on an order
text:
# Updating the email on an order

The email address you enter at checkout is locked to your order and cannot be changed by our support team. Fieldstone uses your email and order number to verify your identity, so changing it would break that security model. If you mistyped your email, a specialist can help you regain access to your order.

title: Updating the email on an order
section_heading: Why the email cannot be changed
text:
# Updating the email on an order

## Why the email cannot be changed

Fieldstone does not use user accounts. Instead, every order is tied to the email address you provided at checkout. When you look up an order or request a return, we send a verification link to that email to confirm it's really you. Changing the email after the fact would allow someone else to hijack your order with only the order number, which is visible on shipping labels and receipts.

Because of this security design, the email is permanent once your order is placed. See our guide on How Fieldstone order numbers work for more about how we verify your identity.

title: Updating the email on an order
section_heading: What to do if you mistyped your email
text:
# Updating the email on an order

## What to do if you mistyped your email

If you entered the wrong email address and cannot receive order updates or return instructions, contact our support team. A specialist will verify your identity using your billing information and the order details, then manually assist with returns, refunds, or shipping questions.

You will not be able to use the self-service order lookup tool, but the specialist can handle everything on your behalf. This process takes one business day for most requests.

title: Updating the email on an order
text:
# Updating the email on an order

## What support can help with

Even though the email on your order cannot be updated, our team can still:

- Process a return or refund manually after verifying your identity
- Resend shipping confirmations to a corrected email address (the original order email stays unchanged in our system)
- Update the shipping address if your order has not shipped yet
- Answer questions about your order status or delivery timeline

For urgent issues like a pending delivery to the wrong address, contact support as soon as possible. Our article on contacting customer support includes response times and what information to have ready.

title: Updating the email on an order
text:
# Updating the email on an order

## FAQ

**Can I update my email for future orders?**  
Yes. Each order is independent, so you can use a different email address the next time you check out. There is no account to update because Fieldstone does not store customer profiles between orders.

**What if I used a work email and no longer have access to it?**  
Contact support with your order number, billing address, and the last four digits of the payment method. A specialist will verify your identity and help you complete your return or answer your question without needing access to the original email.

**Will my order confirmation still go to the wrong email?**  
Yes. The confirmation, shipping updates, and any automated messages will continue to go to the email you entered at checkout. If you need those details, a support specialist can forward them to your corrected address after verifying your identity.

title: About Fieldstone Goods
text:
# About Fieldstone Goods

Fieldstone Goods started in 2019 as a curation project: founder Mara Chen spent six months visiting workshops in Vermont, Pennsylvania, and the Pacific Northwest, asking makers what they would keep in their own kitchens and homes. The first catalog had eleven items. Today we carry a curated selection of kitchen essentials, home textiles, and seasonal goods—each sourced from small producers and built to last years, not seasons.

We use the term "slow-build" to describe how our catalog grows. Instead of monthly drops or trend cycles, we add products only when we find a maker whose work meets our standards for material honesty and functional design. Some items stay in the catalog for years. Others rotate based on seasonal availability or small-batch production limits.

title: About Fieldstone Goods
text:
# About Fieldstone Goods

## What Fieldstone does

We work directly with domestic workshops and small manufacturers. Every product ships from our warehouse in Asheville, North Carolina. Orders are packed by a team of twelve, most of whom have been with us since the beginning.

Our catalog focuses on kitchen tools, natural-fiber textiles, and home goods that serve a clear purpose. We do not carry apparel, electronics, or décor items that exist primarily for visual novelty. When we mark an item final sale, it is usually because the maker produced a limited seasonal run or because we are discontinuing that SKU to make room for something new.

We handle our own order fulfillment, customer support, and returns processing. See our guide on sustainability and packaging for details on how we ship and what materials we use.

title: About Fieldstone Goods
text:
# About Fieldstone Goods

## What Fieldstone does not do

We do not operate fulfillment centers outside the United States. We do not white-label products or source from overseas factories that do not allow site visits. We do not offer same-day delivery, subscription boxes, or influencer collaborations.

We do not have a loyalty program or user accounts in the current version of our shop. You identify yourself using the email address and order number from your confirmation.

Fieldstone does not wholesale to large chains. We do work with independent retailers, design studios, and hospitality clients on trade terms. For those inquiries, see our article on wholesale and trade inquiries.

title: About Fieldstone Goods
text:
# About Fieldstone Goods

## FAQ

**Who owns Fieldstone Goods?**  
Fieldstone is privately owned by Mara Chen and operates as an LLC. We have no outside investors and no parent company.

**Do you have a physical store?**  
We do not have a retail storefront. All orders ship from our Asheville warehouse. Occasionally we participate in regional craft markets; those dates appear on our homepage when scheduled.

**Can I suggest a product or maker?**  
We welcome maker introductions. Email the details through the contact form on our homepage. We review suggestions quarterly but cannot respond to every submission.

title: Accessibility statement
text:
# Accessibility statement

Fieldstone Goods is committed to making our website usable for all customers. We aim to meet Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.1 Level AA standards and continually test with assistive technologies including screen readers, keyboard navigation, and voice control software. If you encounter a barrier while browsing or placing an order, we want to know—and we will help you complete your purchase through an alternative method.

title: Accessibility statement
text:
# Accessibility statement

## Our accessibility standards

We design our site to work with common assistive technologies. This includes:

- **Keyboard navigation**: All interactive elements can be accessed without a mouse.
- **Screen reader support**: Product descriptions, checkout fields, and order confirmations are labeled for clarity with JAWS, NVDA, and VoiceOver.
- **Color contrast**: Text meets WCAG AA contrast ratios against backgrounds.
- **Scalable text**: Pages remain functional when text is resized up to 200% in browser settings.
- **Alternative text**: Product images include descriptive alt text.

We test across browsers and devices, but we know barriers can still appear. When they do, we treat them as bugs and prioritize fixes.

title: Accessibility statement
text:
# Accessibility statement

## Reporting an accessibility issue

If you find a page, feature, or checkout step that does not work with your assistive technology, please let us know. Contact our support team with:

- A description of the barrier (for example, "the promo code field is not announced by my screen reader")
- The page or URL where you encountered it
- The assistive technology and browser you are using

See our guide on **Contacting customer support** for email and chat options. A specialist will respond within one business day. We will work with you to resolve the issue and, if needed, complete your order over the phone or via email.

title: Accessibility statement
text:
# Accessibility statement

## Alternative ordering methods

You can place an order without using the website:

- **Email**: Send your shipping address and the SKUs or product names you want to order. We will reply with a total and payment link.
- **Phone assistance**: A support agent can read product descriptions, check availability, and process payment over the phone during business hours (Monday–Friday, 9 a.m.–6 p.m. EST).

Returns, exchanges, and order tracking are also available through these channels. You will never need to navigate the site to resolve an issue if it is not accessible to you.

title: Accessibility statement
text:
# Accessibility statement

## Ongoing improvements

We review our site quarterly with accessibility consultants and incorporate user feedback into each update. Our current focus areas include improving mobile touch target sizing and adding more detailed product dimension information for assistive technology users.

If you have suggestions beyond bug reports—features that would make shopping easier or content that should be structured differently—we welcome that input as well.

title: Contacting customer support
text:
# Contacting customer support

You can reach Fieldstone support through live chat or email. Chat with Ember, our support agent, for instant help with order tracking, returns, and product questions. For issues that need a specialist—like billing disputes or complex delivery problems—Ember will connect you with the right team.

title: Contacting customer support
text:
# Contacting customer support

## How to contact us

**Live chat** is the fastest way to get help. Click the chat icon in the bottom right corner of any page on fieldstonegoods.com. Ember is available 24/7 and can look up orders, start returns, answer product questions, and help with most order issues on the spot.

**Email** is available if you prefer it or if chat is unavailable. Send your question to support@fieldstonegoods.com. Include your order number (format FG-NNNNNN) and the email address you used at checkout. Response time is typically within 12 hours during business days.

title: Contacting customer support
text:
# Contacting customer support

## Information that speeds things up

Having these details ready helps us resolve your question faster:

- **Order number** — Starts with FG- and appears in your confirmation email. Example: FG-123456.
- **Email address** — The one you used at checkout. We use this to verify your identity.
- **Photos** — If your issue involves damaged items or packaging problems, attach clear photos showing the issue.
- **Tracking number** — For delivery questions, include the tracking number from your shipment confirmation email.

If you are asking about a return, see our guide on [Delivery issues and lost packages](#) for tracking help, or [Duplicate orders and billing questions](#) if you were charged incorrectly.

title: Contacting customer support
text:
# Contacting customer support

## Response windows

**Urgent issues** like missing packages or incorrect items typically get a reply within one hour during business hours (Monday–Friday, 9 AM–6 PM ET). Ember handles most urgent requests immediately in chat.

**Standard questions** about returns, product details, or order changes usually receive a response within 12 hours. If a human specialist needs to review your case—such as for billing disputes or corporate order inquiries—expect a reply within one business day.

**Complex cases** that require warehouse investigation or escalation may take 2–3 business days. We will keep you updated if your issue needs additional time.

title: Contacting customer support
text:
# Contacting customer support

## FAQ

**Can I call Fieldstone support?** We do not currently offer phone support. Chat and email let us research your order thoroughly and provide accurate answers without wait times.

**What if I need help outside business hours?** Ember is available in chat 24/7 and can handle most order questions immediately. Email responses may be delayed until the next business day if sent on weekends or holidays.

**Do I need to create an account to contact support?** No. We verify your identity using your order number and email address, so you can get help without logging in.

title: Press and media requests
text:
# Press and media requests

Fieldstone Goods welcomes editorial coverage, product reviews, and brand collaboration opportunities. Media inquiries are handled by a dedicated team that coordinates interviews, provides product samples, and licenses images for publication. This article explains how to submit a request and what to expect.

title: Press and media requests
text:
# Press and media requests

## Submitting a media inquiry

For all press, editorial, and partnership inquiries, email **press@fieldstonegoodsinc.com** with the following information:

- Your name, publication or platform, and role
- The nature of your request (interview, product review, image licensing, or collaboration)
- Intended publication date or deadline
- Any specific products or topics you want to cover

A member of the media relations team will respond within two business days. If your deadline is urgent, note that in the subject line.

title: Press and media requests
text:
# Press and media requests

## Product samples for editorial review

Writers and content creators working on editorial features may request product samples. Include your media kit or portfolio link, the outlet's audience size or reach, and which specific products you would like to review. Only items from the current catalog are available for sampling.

The team evaluates requests based on editorial focus and alignment with Fieldstone's brand. Approved reviewers receive samples at no charge, with no obligation to publish a positive review. You will receive tracking information once the sample ships, typically within five business days of approval.

title: Press and media requests
text:
# Press and media requests

## Image licensing and brand assets

High-resolution product images, lifestyle photography, and the Fieldstone logo are available for editorial use with prior approval. Requests must include the publication name, story context, and intended usage (print, web, or social media). Images cannot be altered beyond standard cropping and color correction.

Commercial use, including advertising or paid partnerships, requires a separate licensing agreement. Contact the press team to discuss terms.

title: Press and media requests
text:
# Press and media requests

## Interviews and brand storytelling

Journalists writing feature stories or profiles may request interviews with Fieldstone leadership or product specialists. The media team coordinates scheduling and provides background materials. Interview requests should include your story angle, outlet, and preferred timeline. For general background on our company and mission, see our guide on About Fieldstone Goods.

If your inquiry falls outside media relations—such as a customer service question or order issue—visit Contacting customer support to reach the right team.

title: Press and media requests
text:
# Press and media requests

## FAQ

**Can I use Fieldstone product images in a blog post without permission?**  
Editorial bloggers with a clear review or feature context may request image licensing at no charge. Submit a brief description of your post and reach to press@fieldstonegoodsinc.com.

**How long does sample approval take?**  
Most sample requests receive a response within two business days. Approved samples ship within five business days, arriving via standard shipping (3–5 business days).

**Do you sponsor content creators or influencers?**  
Paid partnerships and influencer sponsorships are managed separately from editorial media relations. Include "partnership inquiry" in your email subject line, and the team will direct you to the appropriate contact.

title: Privacy and data practices
text:
# Privacy and data practices

We collect only what we need to fulfill your order and provide support. You have the right to request a copy of your data or ask us to delete it at any time. This article explains what information we collect, how long we keep it, and how to submit a privacy request.

title: Privacy and data practices
text:
# Privacy and data practices

## What information we collect

When you place an order, we collect your email address, shipping address, and payment details. Payment information goes directly to our payment processor and is not stored on our servers. We keep your email and order number (format FG-NNNNNN) to verify your identity when you track a shipment or request a return.

We also collect the browsing activity necessary to complete your transaction, such as the items you added to your cart and the shipping method you selected. We do not use tracking cookies for advertising or sell your information to third parties.

title: Privacy and data practices
section_heading: How long we keep your data
text:
# Privacy and data practices

## How long we keep your data

Order records, including your email and shipping details, are retained for seven years to comply with tax and accounting requirements. After that period, records are automatically deleted unless you have an open return or dispute.

If you contact support, we keep a record of your inquiry and our response for two years. This helps us provide consistent service if you reach out again about the same order. See our guide on contacting customer support for more about how we handle your questions.

title: Privacy and data practices
section_heading: Requesting a copy or deletion of your data
text:
# Privacy and data practices

## Requesting a copy or deletion of your data

You can request a copy of all the personal information we have on file, or ask us to delete it entirely. To submit a privacy request, email your order number and a brief description of what you need to the support address listed in the contacting customer support article. A specialist will verify your identity using the email address on the order and respond within 30 days.

Deletion requests remove your email, shipping address, and support history from our active systems. Order numbers and transaction amounts may be retained in anonymized financial records to meet legal obligations, but they will no longer be linked to your name or contact information.

If you have an open return or refund in progress, we cannot complete a deletion request until that matter is resolved. Once the return window closes and any refund has been processed, deletion can proceed.

title: Privacy and data practices
text:
# Privacy and data practices

## FAQ

**Can I opt out of order confirmation emails?**  
No. Confirmation and shipping emails are transactional and required to complete your purchase. You will not receive marketing emails unless you separately subscribe to our newsletter, which has its own opt-out link in every message.

**Does Fieldstone share data with partners?**  
We share the minimum necessary information with our shipping carrier and payment processor to fulfill your order. We do not share your data with advertisers or data brokers.

**What happens to my data if I never complete checkout?**  
Cart contents are stored temporarily in your browser session and are not linked to any personal information unless you proceed to enter an email address at checkout. Abandoned carts are cleared after 24 hours.

title: Wholesale and trade inquiries
text:
# Wholesale and trade inquiries

Fieldstone Goods partners with independent retailers and design trade professionals who share our commitment to quality home goods. If you're interested in carrying our products in your store or specifying them for client projects, we welcome wholesale and trade inquiries. This article explains who we work with, what to expect, and how to request a line sheet.

title: Wholesale and trade inquiries
text:
# Wholesale and trade inquiries

## Who we partner with

We work with two primary groups: independent retail stores and interior design trade professionals. Independent retailers include specialty home goods shops, lifestyle boutiques, and gift stores that curate collections for local customers. Design trade partners include interior designers, architects, and decorators who specify furnishings and home accessories for residential or commercial projects.

We do not currently work with online-only resellers or drop-ship arrangements. Our wholesale program is built for partners who maintain a physical location or design practice and can present Fieldstone products in a thoughtful context.

title: Wholesale and trade inquiries
text:
# Wholesale and trade inquiries

## Minimum orders and pricing

Wholesale orders have a $500 minimum at wholesale pricing. Opening orders for new accounts typically start at $1,000 to establish a working relationship and ensure a meaningful selection of products. Reorders follow the $500 minimum.

Wholesale pricing is typically 50% off retail for most items. Line sheets include wholesale cost, suggested retail price, and case pack quantities where applicable. Trade pricing for design professionals varies by project scope and is discussed during the inquiry process.

Payment terms for established accounts are net 30. New accounts pay via credit card or bank transfer at the time of order until credit terms are approved.

title: Wholesale and trade inquiries
section_heading: How to request a line sheet
text:
# Wholesale and trade inquiries

## How to request a line sheet

To request a wholesale line sheet or discuss a trade project, contact our wholesale team directly. Because Ember, our support agent, handles individual customer orders and returns, wholesale inquiries go to a human specialist who can discuss pricing, minimums, and partnership details.

Include your business name, location, and a brief description of your retail store or design practice in your inquiry. If you have a website or portfolio, share that as well. A specialist will respond within two business days with a line sheet and next steps.

For corporate gifting or bulk orders that do not require ongoing wholesale terms, see our guide on corporate gifting and wholesale for information on one-time large orders.

title: Wholesale and trade inquiries
text:
# Wholesale and trade inquiries

## FAQ

**Do you offer samples for prospective wholesale accounts?**  
We can arrange sample orders at a discount for serious retail or trade inquiries. The specialist reviewing your request will discuss sample options and any associated costs during your initial conversation.

**Can I carry Fieldstone products if I also sell online?**  
Independent retailers with both physical and online storefronts are welcome to apply. We ask that online resale align with our brand presentation standards, which the wholesale team will review with approved accounts.

**How long does wholesale account approval take?**  
Most inquiries receive a response within two business days. If your business is a good fit, you'll receive a line sheet and account application. Once the application is submitted, approval typically takes three to five business days.

title: Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries
text:
# Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries

Fieldstone Goods welcomes corporate gifting and bulk orders for events, employee appreciation, or client gifts. Orders of 50 units or more require coordination with a human specialist who can discuss custom messaging, volume pricing, and delivery timelines. For smaller bulk purchases (under 50 units), you can place a standard order through the website.

title: Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries
text:
# Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries

## Minimum order quantities

There is no minimum order quantity for regular purchases. You can buy one item or twenty through the standard checkout process.

For corporate gifting programs that involve 50 or more units, custom packaging, or personalized messages at scale, a specialist will work with you directly. These orders cannot be placed through the self-service checkout. The specialist will confirm product availability, coordinate delivery windows, and provide a detailed quote.

Orders between 10 and 49 units can use standard checkout. If you need a custom message included with each item, mention that in the order notes field. Our fulfillment team will do their best to accommodate the request, though we cannot guarantee personalized notes for every unit in that range.

title: Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries
text:
# Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries

## Custom messaging and packaging

Standard orders ship with a Fieldstone Goods packing slip and our signature kraft paper wrap. Custom messages are limited to a single note card per order.

For corporate programs requiring unique messaging for each recipient—such as personalized thank-you notes or individual greeting cards—connect with a specialist. They coordinate these details for orders of 50 units or more. Custom packaging options, branded inserts, or co-branded gift boxes are also available at that volume.

If your company plans to send gifts to multiple addresses (such as remote employees or a client list), the specialist can arrange split shipments from a single bulk order. This avoids placing dozens of separate transactions.

title: Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries
text:
# Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries

## How to request a quote

Corporate and wholesale inquiries route to a human team member who handles pricing, logistics, and custom requests. Ember, our support agent, will connect you with the right specialist when you mention a bulk order or corporate gifting program. See our guide on contacting customer support for the fastest way to reach the team.

When you submit your inquiry, include the following details to speed up the quote process:

- Approximate number of units
- Preferred product SKUs (if known) or a description of what you're looking for
- Delivery timeline or event date
- Whether you need custom messaging, packaging, or split shipments
- Shipping address or addresses

The specialist will typically respond within one business day with a preliminary quote and next steps.

title: Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries
text:
# Corporate gifting and wholesale inquiries

## FAQ

**Can I use a promo code on a corporate order?**  
Promo codes apply to standard checkout orders only. Corporate orders of 50+ units receive volume pricing instead, which the specialist will outline in your quote.

**What if I need the order delivered by a specific date?**  
Mention your deadline when you request a quote. The specialist will confirm whether the timeline is feasible based on current inventory and production capacity. For large orders, we recommend reaching out at least three weeks before your event.

**Are final-sale items available for corporate gifting?**  
Yes. Final-sale products like the Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001) and Seasonal Tea Towels (FG-SAL-002) can be included in bulk orders. Keep in mind that final-sale items cannot be returned, even in a corporate order.

title: Gift cards: purchase and redemption
text:
# Gift cards: purchase and redemption

Fieldstone Goods offers both digital and physical gift cards in denominations from $25 to $500. Digital cards arrive by email within minutes, while physical cards ship in 3–5 business days. Gift cards never expire and can be combined with most promo codes at checkout.

title: Gift cards: purchase and redemption
text:
# Gift cards: purchase and redemption

## Purchasing a gift card

Gift cards are available on our website in $25, $50, $100, $250, and $500 values. Choose digital delivery for instant gifting or select a physical card mailed to your recipient.

When you purchase a digital gift card, the recipient receives an email with a unique redemption code within 15 minutes. You can schedule delivery for a future date if you prefer. Physical gift cards ship to the address you provide using our standard shipping method and arrive in 3–5 business days. Both formats include a personalized message field at checkout.

Gift card purchases are final sale and cannot be returned or exchanged. They also cannot be used to buy additional gift cards.

title: Gift cards: purchase and redemption
text:
# Gift cards: purchase and redemption

## Redeeming a gift card

Enter your gift card code at checkout in the payment section. The code is a 16-character alphanumeric string that appears on the physical card or in the digital delivery email. The balance applies to your order total, including shipping.

If your order exceeds the gift card balance, you can pay the difference with a credit card. If the order total is less than the card value, the remaining balance stays on the gift card for future use. You can check your balance by entering the code on our gift card balance page.

Gift cards apply before promo codes. If you have both, the system deducts the gift card balance first, then applies any active discount to the remaining total. See our guide on promo codes and discounts for details on combining offers.

title: Gift cards: purchase and redemption
text:
# Gift cards: purchase and redemption

## Timing and delivery details

Digital gift cards process immediately after purchase and typically arrive within 15 minutes. Check your spam folder if the email does not appear in the recipient's inbox. Physical cards follow the same 3–5 business day timeline as standard orders.

Gift cards have no expiration date and no fees. If a recipient needs to return a gift purchased with a card, the refund goes back to the original gift card code, not a payment method. For more on returns involving gifts, see our article on gift receipts and gift returns.

title: Gift cards: purchase and redemption
text:
# Gift cards: purchase and redemption

## FAQ

**Can I use multiple gift cards on one order?**  
Yes. Enter each code separately at checkout. The system will apply all valid balances to your order total.

**What happens if I lose a physical gift card?**  
Contact our support team with your original purchase confirmation. A specialist can help reissue the balance, though this process may take 3–5 business days to verify.

**Do gift cards work on final-sale items?**  
Yes. Gift cards apply to any product in our catalog, including items marked final sale.

title: Gift messages and packaging
text:
# Gift messages and packaging

You can add a handwritten gift message at checkout, and we'll include it in the package without showing any prices. All orders use our standard kraft packaging with a cotton ribbon — we do not offer custom gift wrap or boxes at this time.

title: Gift messages and packaging
section_heading: Adding a gift message at checkout
text:
# Gift messages and packaging

## Adding a gift message at checkout

On the shipping page, look for the "Is this a gift?" checkbox. When you check it, a text field appears where you can type your message. Keep your message under 200 characters so it fits on our gift card stock. We print your message on heavyweight paper and tuck it into the package before sealing.

The gift message is optional. If you check "Is this a gift?" without writing anything, we'll still leave the invoice out of the box — you just won't have a card inside.

title: Gift messages and packaging
text:
# Gift messages and packaging

## What gift packaging looks like

Every order ships in our standard kraft box with a cotton ribbon tied around the outside. The box is sturdy, unbranded, and arrives ready to hand off. We do not add decorative paper, bows, tissue, or custom outer wrap.

Inside, items are wrapped in unbleached tissue and packed with recycled fill. If you added a gift message, the printed card sits on top of the tissue.

title: Gift messages and packaging
text:
# Gift messages and packaging

## No invoice in the package

When you mark an order as a gift, we never include a packing slip or invoice with pricing. The recipient sees only the products, the tissue, and your message if you wrote one.

If the recipient needs to return or exchange something, they can use the order number from the shipping confirmation email you received. See our guide on gift receipts and gift returns for details on how that process works.

title: Gift messages and packaging
text:
# Gift messages and packaging

## FAQ

**Can I send the package directly to someone else?**  
Yes. Enter their address as the shipping address at checkout, and make sure to check "Is this a gift?" so no pricing shows up in the box.

**Can I see a preview of the gift card before placing the order?**  
We do not show a preview. Your message is printed exactly as you type it, so double-check spelling and punctuation before you confirm.

**Do you offer gift cards instead of physical products?**  
Yes. We sell digital gift cards in any amount from $25 to $500. The recipient gets an email with a code they can apply at checkout. See our article on gift cards: purchase and redemption for more.

title: Gift receipts and gift returns
text:
# Gift receipts and gift returns

If you received a Fieldstone order as a gift, you can start a return even if you do not have the original purchaser's email address. The refund will be issued to the original payment method, and you will receive store credit equal to the item price.

title: Gift receipts and gift returns
section_heading: Starting a return without the purchaser's email
text:
# Gift receipts and gift returns

## Starting a return without the purchaser's email

Gift recipients do not need the original buyer's email to begin a return. You will need the order number printed on the packing slip included with your shipment. The order number follows the format FG-NNNNNN.

Visit our returns page and enter the order number along with the shipping address where the gift was delivered. Our system will verify the order and display the eligible items. Select the item you wish to return and follow the prompts to generate a prepaid return label.

If your package did not include a packing slip or the order number is unreadable, a human support specialist can look up the order using your shipping address and an approximate delivery date.

title: Gift receipts and gift returns
section_heading: How refunds work for gift returns
text:
# Gift receipts and gift returns

## How refunds work for gift returns

Refunds for gift orders are issued to the original purchaser's payment method. This is a billing system requirement — we cannot redirect refunds to a different card or issue cash equivalents.

As the gift recipient, you will receive a store credit code via email once the warehouse processes your return. The credit matches the full item price and can be used on any future Fieldstone order with no expiration date. The original purchaser will see the refund on their payment method within 5–7 business days of the warehouse receiving your return.

Opened items in original condition are subject to a $7.95 return shipping fee, deducted from the store credit amount. Unopened items return at no cost.

title: Gift receipts and gift returns
text:
# Gift receipts and gift returns

## Gift returns and final-sale items

Items marked as final sale in our catalog cannot be returned, even if received as a gift. Final-sale designation appears on the product page at the time of purchase. Currently, final-sale items include seasonal products such as the Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001) and Seasonal Tea Towels (FG-SAL-002).

If you received a final-sale item as a gift and believe it arrived damaged or defective, contact support with photos of the issue. A specialist will review your case and determine next steps.

title: Gift receipts and gift returns
text:
# Gift receipts and gift returns

## FAQ

**Can I exchange a gift for a different item or size?**  
We do not offer direct exchanges. Return the original item for store credit, then place a new order for the item you prefer. See our guide on how to start a return for step-by-step instructions.

**What if I want to return part of a gift set?**  
Multi-item sets like the Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set (FG-KIT-003) must be returned complete. You cannot return individual pieces from a set and receive partial credit.

**Will the gift giver know I returned their gift?**  
The original purchaser will see the refund transaction on their payment method but will not receive details about which items were returned or why. If you used a gift message or special packaging, visit our guide on gift messages and packaging to learn more about how those features work.

title: Holiday returns: extended window
text:
# Holiday returns: extended window

Orders placed during our holiday shopping period receive an extended return window through mid-January, giving you and your gift recipients extra time. Outside this period, our standard 30-day return policy applies from the delivery date.

title: Holiday returns: extended window
text:
# Holiday returns: extended window

## When the extended window applies

The extended return window covers orders placed between November 1 and December 24. These orders can be returned through January 15 of the following year, regardless of when they arrive. This gives you flexibility for early holiday shopping and ensures gift recipients have time to decide after the new year.

If you place an order outside this date range—before November 1 or after December 24—the standard 30-day return window from delivery applies. For details on how the 30-day period works year-round, see our guide on return eligibility and window.

title: Holiday returns: extended window
text:
# Holiday returns: extended window

## What the extended window covers

All standard return policies still apply during the holiday period. Items must be unopened or in original condition. Final-sale items marked in the catalog, such as the Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001) and Seasonal Tea Towels (FG-SAL-002), cannot be returned even during the extended window.

Return shipping is free for unopened items. Opened items in original condition have a $7.95 return shipping fee. Refunds land on the original payment method within 5–7 business days of the warehouse receiving your return.

title: Holiday returns: extended window
text:
# Holiday returns: extended window

## Starting a holiday return

To start a return, have your order number (format FG-NNNNNN) and the email address used at checkout. The agent Ember will verify your order and generate a prepaid return label if your items are eligible.

Gift recipients can also initiate returns using the order number and email from the original purchase. For more on how gift returns work, including refund destination options, see our article on gift receipts and gift returns.

title: Holiday returns: extended window
text:
# Holiday returns: extended window

## FAQ

**When does the holiday return window end each year?**  
Returns for orders placed November 1 through December 24 are accepted through January 15. Orders placed outside that window follow the standard 30-day policy from delivery.

**Can I return a final-sale item if I bought it during the holidays?**  
No. Final-sale items cannot be returned regardless of purchase date. This includes seasonal products like the Holiday Candle and Seasonal Tea Towels.

**I received a gift in late December. Do I have until January 15 to return it?**  
Yes, as long as the order was placed between November 1 and December 24. The extended window applies to the purchase date, not the delivery date.

title: Promo codes and discounts
text:
# Promo codes and discounts

Fieldstone accepts one promo code per order. Codes apply at checkout and appear as a line-item discount on your order summary. If a code is not working, the most common reasons are expiration, item exclusions, or a minimum purchase requirement that has not been met.

title: Promo codes and discounts
text:
# Promo codes and discounts

## How promo codes work

Enter your code in the promo field at checkout. The discount applies immediately and shows in your order total before payment. You will see the code and discount amount on your order confirmation email.

Only one code can be used per order. If you try to apply a second code, the first one is removed. This includes stacking a percentage-off code with a dollar-off code or combining two separate promotions. Choose the code that gives you the best savings.

Most codes are case-insensitive, but some promotional campaigns require exact entry. If a code is not applying, double-check the spelling and remove any extra spaces.

title: Promo codes and discounts
section_heading: Common reasons a code is invalid
text:
# Promo codes and discounts

## Common reasons a code is invalid

**The code has expired.** Promo codes include an end date in the fine print of the offer. After that date, the code no longer works. Seasonal codes typically expire within a few weeks of the campaign launch.

**Your cart does not meet the minimum purchase requirement.** Some codes require a subtotal of $75 or $100 before the discount applies. Final-sale items and shipping fees do not count toward this threshold.

**The code excludes certain items.** Final-sale products—like our Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001) and Seasonal Tea Towels (FG-SAL-002)—are excluded from most promotions. A few codes also exclude new arrivals or specific product categories. Check the original offer for a list of exclusions.

**You already used a code on this order.** Only one code applies per transaction. If you entered a code earlier in your session, remove it before trying a new one.

title: Promo codes and discounts
text:
# Promo codes and discounts

## Promo pricing and returns

Items purchased with a promo code are eligible for return under our standard 30-day policy. Your refund is based on the discounted price you paid, not the original list price. If you return part of a multi-item order and the remaining items fall below the promo's minimum purchase requirement, the discount is forfeited and you are refunded only the amount you actually paid for the returned items.

For example: you buy three items totaling $120 and apply a code for $20 off orders over $100. You pay $100. If you return one item worth $40 at list price, your refund is calculated from the $100 you paid, not the $120 subtotal.

Holiday orders placed during extended promotional windows follow the same logic. See our guide on Holiday returns: extended window for details on seasonal return deadlines.

title: Promo codes and discounts
text:
# Promo codes and discounts

## FAQ

**Can I apply a promo code to a gift card purchase?**  
No. Gift cards are excluded from promo codes and discounts. See our article Gift cards: purchase and redemption for more on how gift cards work.

**What if my code was valid yesterday but does not work today?**  
The code likely expired overnight. Promo end dates are listed in the original campaign email or on the promotion banner. Once a code expires, it cannot be reactivated.

**Can a human support agent apply a code after I place my order?**  
No. Codes must be entered at checkout. We cannot adjust pricing or apply discounts retroactively. If you forgot to use a code, you may cancel and re-order if your items have not yet shipped.

title: About our linen
text:
# About our linen

Our linen comes from European flax and is woven at a fourth-generation mill in Lithuania. We chose this supply chain because European linen offers proven durability, a naturally textured hand, and environmental advantages over cotton or synthetic fibers. Every linen item in our catalog—including the Linen Throw Blanket (FG-HOM-001)—is made from this same yarn stock.

title: About our linen
text:
# About our linen

## Why European flax

Flax thrives in the temperate, rainy climates of Belgium, France, and the Netherlands, where it needs no irrigation. The crop also requires minimal pesticides and naturally improves soil health between rotations. After harvest, flax stalks are laid in fields to ret—a natural process in which dew and microbes break down the outer stalk, leaving long bast fibers that spinners twist into linen yarn. European growers have refined this technique over centuries, and the result is fiber that is strong, absorbent, and naturally lustrous.

Linen from flax grown in humid maritime climates tends to be finer and more uniform than flax from hotter, drier regions. This matters for items you will wash dozens or hundreds of times. European linen softens with age while maintaining tensile strength, so a blanket that feels crisp when new becomes supple after six months of regular laundering.

title: About our linen
text:
# About our linen

## Our Lithuanian mill partner

We work with a family-owned weaving mill outside Vilnius that has specialized in linen since 1952. The mill sources flax exclusively from cooperatives in Belgium and northern France, spins it into yarn on-site, and weaves it on Jacquard looms that allow precise control over weight and drape. Our throw blanket uses a medium-weight plain weave—sturdy enough for daily use, breathable enough for year-round comfort.

The mill runs quality checks at three stages: raw fiber grading, post-spin yarn testing, and finished fabric inspection. Each bolt is examined for slubs, breaks, and color consistency before it ships to our manufacturing partners. This level of oversight reduces the risk of defects and ensures that every piece of linen we sell meets the same standard.

title: About our linen
section_heading: What to expect from linen goods
text:
# About our linen

## What to expect from linen goods

Linen starts with a slightly crisp, textured feel and softens noticeably after the first few washes. It wrinkles easily, which is part of its character—if you prefer a smooth look, iron while slightly damp. Linen is also naturally temperature-regulating. The hollow core of each flax fiber wicks moisture away from skin in summer and traps warmth in winter, making it ideal for throw blankets and bedding.

Linen may shrink 3–5% in the first wash. For guidance on washing and drying, see our guide on linen washing instructions. For measurements before and after expected shrinkage, check our article on throw blanket dimensions.

title: About our linen
text:
# About our linen

## FAQ

**Does linen pill or wear thin over time?**  
High-quality European linen resists pilling because the long bast fibers lie parallel in the yarn. You may see slight surface fuzz after the first wash, but this disappears as the fabric softens. Linen typically outlasts cotton by a factor of two or three in daily-use items.

**Why does linen cost more than cotton?**  
Flax yields less fiber per acre than cotton, and the retting and spinning processes are more labor-intensive. European linen also carries higher labor and environmental standards than commodity textiles, which adds to the base cost. The tradeoff is longevity—linen goods often last a decade or more with regular use.

title: How our cast iron is made
text:
# How our cast iron is made

The Cast Iron Skillet, 10" (FG-KIT-001) is sand-cast in a Wisconsin foundry using molten iron poured into hand-packed molds. This traditional method creates a heavier, more durable pan than stamped or machined alternatives. Each skillet is pre-seasoned with organic flaxseed oil before it ships, so it arrives ready to cook.

title: How our cast iron is made
text:
# How our cast iron is made

## Sand casting vs. machined iron

Sand casting means liquid iron is poured into a sand mold shaped around a pattern. Once the iron cools, the mold is broken away and the skillet is cleaned and finished. This process produces a thicker wall and base — our 10" skillet weighs just over 5 pounds, compared to 3 or 4 pounds for typical mass-market pans. That extra heft holds heat more evenly and reduces hot spots during searing or frying.

Machined cast iron starts with a flat iron blank that is spun or stamped into shape. It is lighter and faster to produce, but the thinner walls lose heat more quickly and can warp over high heat. Sand-cast iron stays stable across gas, electric, and induction stovetops.

title: How our cast iron is made
text:
# How our cast iron is made

## Why the skillet is heavier

The foundry uses a silica sand blend that allows for thicker walls without weakening the mold. A thicker base stores more thermal energy, which means the pan recovers temperature faster when cold food hits the surface. This is especially useful for browning proteins or cooking multiple batches of cornbread or pancakes.

The trade-off is weight. A 5-pound skillet requires a firm grip and two hands when transferring from stovetop to oven. If you are looking for capacity details or cooking tips, see our Cast Iron Capacity Guide.

title: How our cast iron is made
text:
# How our cast iron is made

## Pre-seasoning at the foundry

After the raw casting is cleaned, it goes through a three-layer seasoning process. Each skillet is coated with organic flaxseed oil and baked at 500°F. The oil polymerizes into a hard, dark patina that protects the iron from rust and provides a naturally nonstick surface. This takes place before the skillet leaves the foundry.

The factory seasoning is durable enough for immediate cooking, but it will continue to improve with use. Fats from cooking build up over time and darken the finish. For step-by-step care instructions, see our guide on Cast Iron Seasoning and Care.

title: How our cast iron is made
text:
# How our cast iron is made

## FAQ

**Does sand-cast iron need special cleaning?**  
No. Wash with hot water and a stiff brush. Avoid soap if possible, though a small amount will not strip the seasoning. Dry immediately and rub with a thin layer of oil after each use.

**Can I use the skillet on induction?**  
Yes. Cast iron works on gas, electric, and induction cooktops. The thick base heats slowly but holds temperature well once hot.

**Will the seasoning wear off?**  
The factory seasoning is permanent unless you strip it with harsh chemicals or leave the pan wet for days. Normal cooking and cleaning will not remove it. Light rust can be scrubbed off and re-seasoned at home.

title: How our ceramics are made
text:
# How our ceramics are made

Every ceramic piece in the Fieldstone catalog starts as raw stoneware clay and goes through a controlled firing process that produces smooth, durable surfaces. We inspect each piece under raking light before it leaves the studio, catching micro-cracks and glaze defects that compromise performance. This article explains the clay body we use, the kiln temperature that creates strength, and the quality check that ensures you receive ceramics built to last.

title: How our ceramics are made
text:
# How our ceramics are made

## The stoneware clay body

Fieldstone ceramics use a mid-range stoneware formulation with a tight particle structure. Stoneware fires denser than earthenware, which means lower water absorption and better resistance to thermal shock. The raw clay arrives as dry powder, mixed with water to a workable consistency, then aged for several weeks to improve plasticity. Aging allows water molecules to penetrate evenly, which reduces warping during the first firing.

Our Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set (FG-KIT-003) and Espresso Cups (FG-KIT-004) both use this stoneware body. The clay's natural iron content gives the unglazed foot a warm taupe color. That unglazed ring also protects countertops and table surfaces from glaze abrasion.

title: How our ceramics are made
text:
# How our ceramics are made

## Kiln firing at Cone 6

All Fieldstone ceramics fire to Cone 6, approximately 2,232°F. This temperature fully vitrifies the clay body, meaning the particles fuse into a glass-like matrix without melting the piece into slag. Vitrification is what makes stoneware non-porous and dishwasher-safe. The firing cycle takes fourteen hours: a slow ramp to avoid thermal shock, a twenty-minute hold at peak temperature, and a controlled cool-down that prevents dunting cracks.

Glaze application happens before the final firing. We use food-safe glazes that mature at the same Cone 6 temperature, creating a permanent bond between glaze and clay. The glaze layer seals the surface and provides the smooth finish you see on mixing bowls and espresso cups. For more on how that glaze holds up in a dishwasher, see our guide on ceramic cookware and dishwasher use.

title: How our ceramics are made
text:
# How our ceramics are made

## Quality inspection for micro-cracks

After the kiln cools, each piece goes through a visual and acoustic inspection. An inspector examines the piece under angled LED light, which casts shadows into hairline cracks invisible under direct overhead light. Raking light reveals glaze crawling, pinholing, and S-cracks that form during cooling.

The inspector also taps the rim with a wooden dowel. A clear ring indicates structural integrity. A dull thud means an internal crack, and that piece does not ship. Micro-cracks weaken ceramics and can widen with thermal cycling, eventually causing a sudden break. This inspection step is why defect returns are rare, but if a piece does arrive damaged, our standard return window applies.

title: How our ceramics are made
section_heading: Why firing temperature matters to you
text:
# How our ceramics are made

## Why firing temperature matters to you

Cone 6 stoneware sits in a functional sweet spot. It is strong enough for daily use, dishwasher-safe, and less brittle than high-fire porcelain. You can move a Fieldstone mixing bowl from refrigerator to counter without worrying about thermal shock. The vitrified body does not absorb food oils or stains, which keeps the ceramic hygienic over years of use.

If you need capacity details for meal prep or baking, see our article on mixing bowl set volumes for dimensions and nesting specs.

title: How our ceramics are made
text:
# How our ceramics are made

## FAQ

**Can I put Fieldstone ceramics in the oven?**  
Yes. Cone 6 stoneware is oven-safe up to 450°F. Avoid direct stovetop heat and do not place a cold ceramic dish into a preheated oven—let it warm gradually to prevent shock cracks.

**Why does the bottom of my bowl feel rough?**  
The unglazed foot ring prevents glaze-on-glaze scratching when bowls are stacked. That raw stoneware texture is intentional and will not damage surfaces.

**Do you offer replacement pieces for broken items in a set?**  
We do not sell individual replacements. If a piece from the Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set arrives damaged, you can return the complete set within thirty days for a refund.

title: Sustainability and packaging
text:
# Sustainability and packaging

Fieldstone Goods uses 100% recycled corrugate for shipping boxes and chooses minimal packaging to reduce waste. We do not include paper filler in most shipments because cushioning adds transport weight and emissions without protecting products that already arrive wrapped. Expedited shipping carries a higher carbon cost per item due to less efficient routing and partial truck loads.

title: Sustainability and packaging
section_heading: What our shipping boxes are made from
text:
# Sustainability and packaging

## What our shipping boxes are made from

Every box that leaves our warehouse is made from post-consumer recycled corrugate. The outer brown layer comes from previously used cardboard that has been pulped and reformed. Box strength meets industry standards for parcel shipment, and the material itself can be recycled again in your curbside bin after use.

We print shipping labels with soy-based ink and use water-activated tape, which bonds directly to the corrugate and does not interfere with the recycling stream. Plastic packing tape would need to be removed before the box could be processed.

title: Sustainability and packaging
section_heading: Why we skip the filler paper
text:
# Sustainability and packaging

## Why we skip the filler paper

Many retailers add crinkle paper, shredded newsprint, or kraft cushioning to fill empty space in a box. Fieldstone does not include these materials in standard shipments. Each item in the catalog arrives wrapped in its original protective sleeve or bag, which provides enough cushioning for transit. Adding loose fill increases the package's dimensional weight, which in turn increases fuel consumption per delivery and raises the shipment's overall carbon footprint.

If an item is fragile or irregularly shaped, we use only the amount of corrugated insert or wrap needed to prevent shifting. We do not add filler for aesthetic reasons or to create an unboxing experience.

title: Sustainability and packaging
text:
# Sustainability and packaging

## Carbon trade-offs in expedited shipping

Standard ground shipping consolidates packages into full truckloads that travel along optimized routes. This method uses less fuel per item and produces fewer emissions. Expedited shipping requires partial loads, priority aircraft cargo, and hub-and-spoke networks that move smaller batches more frequently. The result is a higher carbon cost per package.

We offer expedited delivery for time-sensitive orders, but standard shipping remains the lower-impact choice when delivery speed is flexible. See our guide on Shipping methods and timelines for transit windows and cutoff times.

title: Sustainability and packaging
text:
# Sustainability and packaging

## Material sourcing across the catalog

Packaging choices are one part of a larger effort to source responsibly. Wool products in the catalog come from certified farms that meet animal welfare and land stewardship standards. See Wool sourcing and certifications for details on what those standards require and how suppliers are audited.

Linen, ceramic, and wood items are made by workshops that prioritize natural materials and minimal chemical finishing. We list the origin country and primary material for each product on its catalog page.

title: Sustainability and packaging
text:
# Sustainability and packaging

## FAQ

**Can I request plastic-free packaging?**  
All standard shipments are already plastic-free except for the protective sleeve around wool and linen items, which prevents moisture damage in transit. We cannot remove that sleeve without risking product loss.

**What should I do with the box after delivery?**  
Break down the box flat and place it in your curbside recycling bin. Remove the shipping label if your hauler requires it, though most facilities screen out paper labels during processing.

**Does expedited shipping use different packaging?**  
No. Expedited and standard orders use the same recycled corrugate boxes. The only difference is routing speed and transport method.

title: Where our wood comes from
text:
# Where our wood comes from

The walnut in our Walnut Cutting Board comes from responsibly managed forests in the Appalachian region of the United States. Every board is milled from FSC-certified lumber, edge-glued, sanded to a food-safe finish, and treated with mineral oil before it ships. The price reflects both the quality of the raw material and the craft required to turn rough lumber into a durable kitchen tool.

title: Where our wood comes from
text:
# Where our wood comes from

## The walnut source

We source black walnut (Juglans nigra) from FSC-certified sawmills in Pennsylvania, West Virginia, and Ohio. FSC certification guarantees that the wood comes from forests managed for biodiversity, minimal ecological impact, and fair labor practices. Each board's lumber is kiln-dried to 6–8% moisture content to prevent warping and cracking once the board enters your kitchen.

Black walnut is prized for its rich color, tight grain, and natural resistance to bacteria. The boards vary slightly in tone — some lean darker chocolate, others show lighter sapwood streaks — because every tree is unique.

title: Where our wood comes from
section_heading: From rough lumber to finished board
text:
# Where our wood comes from

## From rough lumber to finished board

A cutting board is not simply a slice of log. The manufacturing process involves several steps:

- **Selection and grading**: Only premium-grade planks free of knots, splits, and sapwood inclusions make it into production.
- **Edge gluing**: Narrow strips are glued side by side with food-safe adhesive to create a stable, wide surface. Edge grain construction resists knife marks better than face grain.
- **Milling and sanding**: The glued slab is planed flat, cut to dimension, and sanded through progressively finer grits until smooth.
- **Oiling**: Each board receives multiple coats of USP-grade mineral oil to seal the wood and bring out its natural color.

This process takes about two weeks per production batch. See our guide on cutting board maintenance for tips on keeping the finish in good condition.

title: Where our wood comes from
text:
# Where our wood comes from

## What the price reflects

The $38.00 price of the Walnut Cutting Board accounts for certified raw material, skilled labor, and quality control. FSC-certified walnut costs 20–30% more than conventional lumber. Edge-glued construction requires precision joinery. Each board is inspected for flatness, finish consistency, and structural integrity before it ships.

You are paying for a tool that will last decades if properly maintained. For long-term care instructions, including how to re-oil the surface as it ages, see our guide on oiled wood long-term care.

title: Where our wood comes from
text:
# Where our wood comes from

## FAQ

**Does the board come with documentation of FSC certification?**  
The board itself does not ship with a certificate, but our supplier chain-of-custody records are available on request. Contact us with your order number if you need documentation for a project or institution.

**Why does my board look different from the one in the product photo?**  
Natural wood varies. Some boards have darker heartwood throughout, others show lighter sapwood edges or subtle mineral streaks. All variations meet our grading standards and perform identically in the kitchen.

title: Wool sourcing and certifications
text:
# Wool sourcing and certifications

Our Wool Area Rug uses fiber from farms certified under the Responsible Wool Standard. That means the sheep are treated humanely, the land is managed to prevent overgrazing, and the supply chain is traceable from pasture to finished textile. Certified wool typically costs 15–25% more than conventional fiber, but it gives you transparency about animal welfare and environmental impact.

title: Wool sourcing and certifications
text:
# Wool sourcing and certifications

## What RWS certification covers

The Responsible Wool Standard (RWS) is an independent certification administered by the Textile Exchange. Farms earning RWS status must meet requirements in three areas:

- **Animal welfare**: Access to pasture, shelter from extreme weather, and veterinary care. Practices like mulesing (a painful skin-removal technique) are prohibited.
- **Land management**: Ranchers follow rotational grazing plans to protect soil health and native vegetation. The standard also requires biodiversity protections and limits pesticide use.
- **Chain of custody**: Every handler — from shearing shed to processor to dye house — tracks RWS fiber separately from conventional wool, ensuring what you buy actually came from a certified source.

Third-party auditors inspect farms and processors annually. If a supplier falls out of compliance, they lose the certification until corrective action is verified.

title: Wool sourcing and certifications
text:
# Wool sourcing and certifications

## Where the fiber originates

The wool in our 5x7 rug (FG-HOM-002) comes from ranches in New Zealand and Tasmania. Both regions have long wool-growing traditions and strong regulatory frameworks for animal husbandry. We work with a single mill in New Zealand that sources only RWS fiber and handles dyeing, spinning, and weaving in one location. That consolidation reduces transport emissions and gives us tighter quality control than a multi-stage global supply chain.

The finished rugs ship to our warehouse in the continental U.S., where they're inspected and stored flat to preserve the weave. For sizing recommendations and room-layout tips, see our Rug sizes and fit guide.

title: Wool sourcing and certifications
text:
# Wool sourcing and certifications

## Why certified wool costs more
