RWS-certified fiber commands a premium because the certification itself has costs — audit fees, separate handling at the mill, smaller production runs — and because responsible land management typically yields less wool per acre than intensive grazing. You're also paying for traceability. Conventional wool often mixes fiber from dozens of farms across multiple countries, making it impossible to verify how the animals were treated or whether the land was degraded in the process.

We absorb some of that premium, but a rug made from certified wool will always cost more than one using untraced fiber. The trade-off is confidence that your purchase supports ranchers who meet higher welfare and environmental standards.

Wool is durable and naturally stain-resistant, so a well-maintained rug can last decades. Our Wool rug cleaning guide walks through vacuuming schedules, spot treatment, and when to call a professional cleaner.

title: Wool sourcing and certifications
text:
# Wool sourcing and certifications

## FAQ

**Does RWS certification mean the wool is organic?**  
No. Organic certification covers pesticide and fertilizer use on pasture, plus restrictions on medications given to sheep. RWS focuses on animal welfare and land stewardship but does not prohibit synthetic inputs. Some farms hold both certifications, but ours currently meet RWS standards only.

**Can I request wool from a specific country or ranch?**  
We source from a single mill that aggregates fiber from multiple RWS ranches in New Zealand and Tasmania. We cannot trace an individual rug back to one farm, but the mill provides quarterly supply-chain reports that list all certified suppliers for that production window.

**What happens to wool that doesn't meet certification standards?**  
It enters the conventional wool market, where it's sold without welfare or land-management claims. RWS farms can produce both certified and non-certified fiber if only part of their operation meets the standard — the two streams are kept separate through the supply chain.

title: Delivery issues and lost packages
text:
# Delivery issues and lost packages

If your tracking shows delivered but you don't have your package, or if your order seems stuck in transit, start by waiting 48 hours. Carriers sometimes scan packages early or deliver to a secure location you haven't checked yet. Most packages appear within two days of a "delivered" scan.

title: Delivery issues and lost packages
section_heading: When tracking shows delivered but you don't have the package
text:
# Delivery issues and lost packages

## When tracking shows delivered but you don't have the package

Check these spots first:

- **Front porch, back porch, or side entrance.** Drivers often leave packages where they're least visible from the street.
- **Mailroom, leasing office, or building concierge.** Apartment and condo deliveries are frequently signed for by staff.
- **Behind planters, furniture, or inside a screen door.** Drivers tuck packages out of sight for security.
- **With a neighbor.** Some carriers deliver to an adjacent address if yours seems inaccessible.

Confirm the delivery address on your order confirmation email matches where you expected the package. If you recently moved or entered an old address at checkout, the package may have gone to the wrong location.

Wait 48 hours from the "delivered" timestamp. Scans occasionally run ahead of the actual drop-off, especially late in the day. See our guide on Tracking your order for more on how to read tracking events.

title: Delivery issues and lost packages
section_heading: When your package is delayed in transit
text:
# Delivery issues and lost packages

## When your package is delayed in transit

Shipments sometimes pause at regional hubs during weather events, peak volume periods, or carrier staffing issues. If tracking hasn't updated in three or more business days, the delay is likely on the carrier's side.

Standard shipping takes 3–5 business days from the ship date. Expedited shipping takes 1–2 business days. Count only business days — weekends and federal holidays don't apply.

Check the latest scan location in your tracking details. If the package is still moving between facilities, it's in the carrier network and should resume normal transit soon.

title: Delivery issues and lost packages
text:
# Delivery issues and lost packages

## When to contact support

Reach out to our team if:

- 48 hours have passed since a "delivered" scan and you've checked all possible locations
- Tracking shows no movement for five or more business days
- The package was delivered to the wrong address and you cannot retrieve it
- Tracking indicates the package is lost or damaged in transit

When you contact us, have your order number (format FG-NNNNNN) and the email address used at checkout ready. We'll open a claim with the carrier and work toward a resolution, which may include a replacement shipment or refund depending on the situation.

You can reach support through the contact form on our site — see Contacting customer support for details.

title: Delivery issues and lost packages
text:
# Delivery issues and lost packages

## If the package was stolen

If you confirmed delivery to the correct address but believe the package was stolen, file a report with your local police department and provide us with the report number when you contact support. We'll use that information as part of the carrier claim process.

Some home insurance or renters insurance policies cover stolen deliveries — check your policy if the order value is significant.

title: Delivery issues and lost packages
text:
# Delivery issues and lost packages

## FAQ

**Can I reroute a package that's already shipped?**  
No. Once the carrier picks up your order, we cannot change the delivery address or hold it at a facility. If you need to intercept a shipment, contact the carrier directly using the tracking number, though they may charge a fee and success is not guaranteed.

**What if only part of my order arrived?**  
Larger orders sometimes ship in multiple boxes with separate tracking numbers. Check your shipping confirmation email for all tracking links. If you've received all tracked packages and items are still missing, contact support with your order number and a list of what didn't arrive.

**Does Fieldstone replace lost packages?**  
We work with the carrier to investigate and resolve delivery issues. Replacement or refund depends on the outcome of the carrier claim and the specific circumstances. We'll keep you updated throughout the process.

title: International shipping
text:
# International shipping

Fieldstone Goods currently ships to the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, and Australia. We handle the shipping logistics, but you are responsible for any customs duties, taxes, or fees your country requires. International orders typically arrive within 7–14 business days, depending on your location and customs processing times.

title: International shipping
text:
# International shipping

## Where we ship

We ship to these countries:

- United States (all 50 states)
- Canada
- United Kingdom
- Australia

We do not ship to PO boxes or military APO/FPO addresses outside the US. If your shipping address falls outside these four countries, you cannot complete checkout at this time.

title: International shipping
text:
# International shipping

## Customs duties and taxes

You pay any customs duties, import taxes, or brokerage fees that your country assesses when the package arrives. These charges are separate from your Fieldstone order total and are collected by the courier or customs agency at delivery.

We cannot estimate these fees in advance because they vary by country, product category, and declared value. Your order confirmation email shows only the merchandise cost and our shipping charge. Check with your local customs authority if you need an estimate before ordering.

The package will include a commercial invoice showing the item value. Do not ask us to mark the shipment as a gift or declare a lower value — we cannot alter customs paperwork.

title: International shipping
text:
# International shipping

## Shipping timelines

International delivery takes 7–14 business days after your order ships. See our guide on shipping methods and delivery timelines for more about when orders leave our warehouse.

Transit time depends on:

- Your country and region
- Customs clearance speed
- Local courier delivery schedules

You'll receive a tracking number by email when your order ships. Tracking updates may pause for a few days while the package clears customs. This is normal.

title: International shipping
text:
# International shipping

## Returns from outside the US

The 30-day return window applies to international orders. You pay return shipping costs to send items back to our US warehouse.

If the item is unopened and in original condition, you receive a full refund of the merchandise cost once we process the return. Our original shipping charge and your return shipping cost are not refundable. Opened items that meet our return criteria have the standard $7.95 return processing fee deducted from the refund. See return shipping fees for details on how that fee applies.

Use a trackable shipping method when returning items internationally. We cannot issue a refund until the package arrives at our warehouse. Customs fees you paid when receiving your order are not refundable — contact your country's customs authority if you have questions about recovering those charges.

Final-sale items cannot be returned, even on international orders.

title: International shipping
text:
# International shipping

## FAQ

**Can I use expedited shipping for international orders?**  
No. Only standard international shipping is available. Delivery takes 7–14 business days regardless of the shipping option you select at checkout.

**What if my package is held in customs?**  
Contact your local courier or customs office with your tracking number. We cannot intervene in customs processes or expedite clearance. If the package is returned to us as undeliverable, we will refund your merchandise cost minus our original shipping charge.

**Do you ship to my country if it's not listed?**  
Not at this time. We ship only to the United States, Canada, the United Kingdom, and Australia. We cannot make exceptions or arrange special shipping to other destinations.

title: Order confirmation emails
text:
# Order confirmation emails

You receive an order confirmation email within minutes of placing your order. It includes your order number, items purchased, total amount, and estimated delivery date. If the email doesn't arrive, check your spam folder or use your email address to retrieve the order details.

title: Order confirmation emails
text:
# Order confirmation emails

## What's in your confirmation email

The confirmation email arrives from orders@fieldstonegoods.com with the subject line "Your Fieldstone order is confirmed." It contains:

- Your order number (format FG-NNNNNN)
- A list of items with SKUs and individual prices
- Shipping address and method
- Order total and payment method (last four digits)
- Estimated delivery date

The order number is your primary reference for tracking, returns, and support requests. Store this email or note the order number somewhere accessible.

title: Order confirmation emails
section_heading: If you don't receive the email
text:
# Order confirmation emails

## If you don't receive the email

Check your spam or promotions folder first. Email filters sometimes catch order confirmations. Search for "fieldstonegoods.com" or "order confirmed" to locate it.

If the email still doesn't appear after 30 minutes, the order likely did not complete. Common reasons include payment authorization failures or session timeouts. Try placing your order again. If the second attempt also produces no confirmation, contact Ember for help.

You cannot resend a confirmation email to a different address after placing an order. The confirmation always goes to the email address entered at checkout.

title: Order confirmation emails
text:
# Order confirmation emails

## Retrieving a lost order number

If you deleted the confirmation email or cannot locate it, you can retrieve your order details using your email address and basic order information. See our guide on finding your order number for step-by-step instructions.

You'll need your email address and an approximate order date or item name. The system matches these details to orders in our database and displays your order number and current status. For tracking updates once you have the order number, see Tracking your order.

title: Order confirmation emails
text:
# Order confirmation emails

## FAQ

**Can I change the email address after placing an order?**  
No. The confirmation email and all order updates go to the address entered at checkout. If you need to update contact information for a pending order, Ember can help.

**What if I receive multiple confirmation emails for one order?**  
You should receive exactly one confirmation. Multiple emails may indicate duplicate orders. Check each order number to verify. If you see charges for orders you did not intend to place, contact support immediately.

**Do I get another email when the order ships?**  
Yes. You'll receive a shipping notification with tracking information once your order leaves the warehouse, typically within 1–2 business days of the confirmation email.

title: Shipping methods and delivery timelines
text:
# Shipping methods and delivery timelines

Fieldstone Goods offers two shipping options: standard delivery (3–5 business days) and expedited delivery (1–2 business days). Both options ship from our warehouse in the continental United States and include tracking from the moment your order leaves our facility. We do not offer same-day delivery.

title: Shipping methods and delivery timelines
text:
# Shipping methods and delivery timelines

## Standard shipping

Standard shipping takes 3–5 business days after your order ships. This method works well for most orders and covers the full continental United States. Once your order leaves the warehouse, you will receive a tracking number by email so you can follow its progress. Business days exclude weekends and major holidays.

Orders placed before 2:00 PM Eastern on a business day typically ship the same day. Orders placed after that cutoff ship the next business day. See our guide on when will my order ship for more on processing timelines.

title: Shipping methods and delivery timelines
text:
# Shipping methods and delivery timelines

## Expedited shipping

Expedited shipping delivers in 1–2 business days after your order ships. Choose this option at checkout if you need something faster. The same 2:00 PM Eastern cutoff applies: orders placed before that time on a business day usually ship the same day, and orders placed after ship the next business day.

Expedited shipping costs more than standard, and the exact rate depends on your delivery address. You will see the cost before you confirm your order.

title: Shipping methods and delivery timelines
section_heading: Why we do not offer same-day delivery
text:
# Shipping methods and delivery timelines

## Why we do not offer same-day delivery

Same-day delivery requires local inventory hubs and fulfillment infrastructure that we do not currently operate. Our fulfillment model centers on a single warehouse that ships nationwide, which lets us maintain quality control and competitive pricing. Expedited shipping remains the fastest option we can reliably offer across all regions.

title: Shipping methods and delivery timelines
text:
# Shipping methods and delivery timelines

## Tracking and international orders

All domestic orders include tracking. You can monitor your shipment through the link in your shipping confirmation email or by visiting the tracking your order page. For addresses outside the continental United States, delivery timelines and costs vary. Review our international shipping article for details on customs, duties, and expected transit times.

title: Shipping methods and delivery timelines
text:
# Shipping methods and delivery timelines

## FAQ

**Can I upgrade my order to expedited shipping after I place it?**  
You cannot change the shipping method once an order is confirmed. If your order has not yet shipped, contact us and we can cancel the original order so you can reorder with expedited shipping.

**What happens if my order does not arrive within the estimated window?**  
Delays occasionally occur due to carrier issues or weather. Check your tracking information first. If the tracking shows no movement for several days or the package appears lost, reach out and we will work with the carrier to resolve it.

**Do business days include Saturday?**  
No. Business days are Monday through Friday, excluding federal holidays. A standard order placed Thursday evening, for example, typically ships Friday and arrives the following Wednesday or Thursday.

title: Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses
text:
# Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses

We ship to most PO boxes and military addresses, but some items cannot reach these locations due to carrier restrictions. Standard shipping works for all eligible orders, while expedited shipping is unavailable for PO box and military destinations.

title: Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses
section_heading: What we ship to PO boxes
text:
# Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses

## What we ship to PO boxes

We can send orders to domestic PO boxes when the package fits the box dimensions and contains only items that ship via USPS. If your order includes only smaller items like the Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set (FG-KIT-003), Espresso Cups (FG-KIT-004), or Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001), standard shipping to a PO box works fine.

Larger items ship via freight or specialty carriers that cannot deliver to PO boxes. The Wool Area Rug, 5x7 (FG-HOM-002) always ships freight and cannot be delivered to a PO box address. If your cart includes a rug or other oversized item, you'll need to provide a physical street address at checkout.

Only standard shipping (3–5 business days) is available for PO box addresses. Expedited shipping requires a street address.

title: Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses
section_heading: Shipping to APO and FPO addresses
text:
# Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses

## Shipping to APO and FPO addresses

We ship to military addresses (APO, FPO, and DPO) using USPS. Standard shipping (3–5 business days domestic, 7–14 business days international) is the only method available for these destinations. Expedited shipping is not offered.

The same size restrictions apply: if an item ships via freight carrier, it cannot be delivered to an APO or FPO address. The Wool Area Rug, 5x7 (FG-HOM-002) cannot ship to military addresses. All other current catalog items are eligible.

Military addresses follow the same return policy as domestic orders. You have 30 days from delivery to return eligible items, though return shipping times may be longer. See our guide on shipping methods and delivery timelines for more about transit expectations.

title: Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses
text:
# Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses

## Address format at checkout

Enter your PO box or military address exactly as it appears in USPS records. For PO boxes, use the format "PO Box [number]" in the street address field. For military addresses, use the full APO, FPO, or DPO designation with the correct AA/AE/AP state code and ZIP code.

If the address format isn't recognized at checkout or if your order includes an oversized item, you'll see an error message before payment. Double-check the address formatting or remove any freight items from your cart.

title: Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses
text:
# Shipping to PO boxes, APO, and FPO addresses

## FAQ

**Can I use expedited shipping to a PO box?** No. Expedited shipping (1–2 business days) requires a physical street address. Only standard shipping is available for PO box and military addresses.

**Why can't the rug ship to my APO address?** The Wool Area Rug, 5x7 ships via freight carrier due to size and weight. Freight carriers cannot deliver to PO boxes or military addresses. Other items in our catalog ship USPS and work with APO/FPO destinations.

**Will my APO order take longer than domestic orders?** Military addresses overseas typically see 7–14 business day delivery windows instead of the standard 3–5 days. Domestic APO addresses follow the same timeline as other U.S. deliveries.

title: Tracking your order
text:
# Tracking your order

Your tracking link arrives by email within one business day of order placement. Check your shipment's location, see what each status means, and know when to reach out if tracking stops updating.

title: Tracking your order
text:
# Tracking your order

## Finding your tracking link

You'll receive a shipping confirmation email from Fieldstone Goods once your order leaves the warehouse. This email contains your tracking number and a direct link to the carrier's tracking page. The subject line will read "Your Fieldstone order has shipped." If you don't see it in your inbox, check your spam or promotions folder—some email providers redirect order confirmations automatically.

The tracking number is also embedded in the link itself, so you can copy it and paste it directly into the carrier's website if the link doesn't work for any reason. Your shipping confirmation email also restates your estimated delivery window based on the shipping method you selected at checkout. See our guide on shipping methods and delivery timelines for details on standard versus expedited options.

title: Tracking your order
text:
# Tracking your order

## Understanding tracking statuses

Carrier tracking systems use a handful of common statuses. Here's what each one means:

**Label created.** The warehouse has printed a shipping label and the carrier has received the package details electronically. The physical package is waiting for pickup. This status typically lasts a few hours.

**In transit.** The carrier has the package and it's moving through their distribution network. Most standard shipments show this status for two to four days.

**Out for delivery.** The package is on a truck and will arrive by end of business day. Delivery times vary by route—residential deliveries often arrive between 9 a.m. and 8 p.m.

**Delivered.** The carrier has left the package at your address. If you didn't receive it, check common drop-off spots like a porch, mailroom, or with a building manager. For persistent issues, our article on delivery issues and lost packages walks through next steps.

**Exception.** The carrier encountered a delay—weather, incorrect address, access issues. Check the tracking page for notes. Most exceptions resolve within 24 hours, and the package resumes normal transit.

title: Tracking your order
section_heading: What to do when tracking stalls
text:
# Tracking your order

## What to do when tracking stalls

If your tracking hasn't updated in more than 48 hours and the package is still marked "in transit," the carrier may be experiencing a backlog or a scanning issue. This happens occasionally, especially during peak seasons.

Wait one additional business day. Many packages arrive on time even when tracking lags. If the package still hasn't moved after 72 hours total, or if you're past your estimated delivery date, contact Ember, our support agent. Have your order number (format FG-NNNNNN) and the email address you used at checkout ready. Ember can verify delivery status with the carrier and, if the package is confirmed lost, start the replacement or refund process.

Tracking sometimes updates late in the evening or overnight, so refresh the page the following morning before escalating.

title: Tracking your order
text:
# Tracking your order

## FAQ

**Can I change the delivery address after the order ships?**  
No. Once the carrier has the package, the address cannot be changed. If you need the order sent elsewhere, you can refuse delivery when it arrives, and it will return to Fieldstone. Contact Ember to arrange a refund or reshipment to the correct address once the return is processed.

**Why does tracking show "delivered" but I don't have the package?**  
Check with neighbors, your building's front desk, or any household members who may have brought it inside. Carriers sometimes mark delivery a few hours early. If 24 hours pass and the package doesn't appear, reach out to Ember with your order number—we'll file a claim with the carrier.

**Does Fieldstone use multiple carriers?**  
We work with a primary national carrier for most shipments. Your shipping confirmation email identifies which carrier is handling your order. Tracking behavior and delivery windows can vary slightly depending on the carrier and your location.

title: When will my order ship
text:
# When will my order ship

Most orders ship within one business day of placement. Orders placed after 2 PM Eastern or on weekends ship the next business day. You'll receive a shipping confirmation email with tracking details once your order leaves our warehouse.

title: When will my order ship
text:
# When will my order ship

## Order processing timeline

We process orders Monday through Friday, excluding federal holidays. Orders placed before 2 PM Eastern on a business day typically ship the same day. Orders placed after 2 PM ship the following business day.

Weekend orders ship on Monday. If you order Friday evening, your package will not ship until Monday at the earliest. The same applies to orders placed on federal holidays — they ship on the next business day.

Processing time is separate from delivery time. Standard shipping takes 3–5 business days after your order ships, while expedited shipping takes 1–2 business days. For complete details on delivery windows, see our guide on shipping methods and delivery timelines.

title: When will my order ship
text:
# When will my order ship

## Reading your order status

You can check your order status anytime by entering your email and order number on our order lookup page. Here's what each status means:

**Processing** — We've received your order and are preparing it for shipment. Most orders stay in this status for a few hours.

**Shipped** — Your order has left our warehouse and is with the carrier. You'll receive a shipping confirmation email with your tracking number. Use that number to follow your package in real time. Our article on tracking your order walks you through the process.

**Delivered** — The carrier has delivered your package to the address on file.

title: When will my order ship
text:
# When will my order ship

## What delays shipping

A few situations can extend processing time beyond one business day. High-demand periods like holidays may add an extra day. Incorrect or incomplete shipping addresses require manual verification, which delays handoff to the carrier.

Payment issues also pause shipment. If your payment method is declined, we'll email you at the address on file. Once you update your payment information, your order re-enters the processing queue.

title: When will my order ship
text:
# When will my order ship

## FAQ

**Can I change my shipping speed after I place my order?**  
If your order status is still "Processing," contact us right away. We can sometimes adjust shipping speed before the package leaves the warehouse, though we cannot guarantee it. Once the status changes to "Shipped," the carrier speed is locked.

**Will I get an email when my order ships?**  
Yes. You'll receive a shipping confirmation email with your tracking number and an estimated delivery date. If you do not see this email within two business days of ordering, check your spam folder or look up your order status directly. For more on what to expect, review our article on order confirmation emails.

**Do you ship on Saturdays?**  
No. Our warehouse operates Monday through Friday. Orders ship on business days only.

title: Candle safety and burn tips
text:
# Candle safety and burn tips

Our Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001) is hand-poured with a soy-blend wax and designed to burn cleanly for 40–50 hours. Following a few simple practices during the first burn and throughout the candle's life will prevent tunneling, extend burn time, and keep the flame safe and steady.

title: Candle safety and burn tips
text:
# Candle safety and burn tips

## First burn matters most

The first time you light the candle sets the wax memory. Let the melt pool reach the edge of the container before extinguishing — typically 2 to 3 hours for our 3.5-inch diameter vessel. If you blow out the flame early, the wax will tunnel on every subsequent burn, wasting product and shortening the candle's life. Plan your first session when you can monitor the candle without interruption.

Never burn for more than 4 hours at a time. Longer sessions overheat the container and can cause the wick to mushroom or the flame to flicker unevenly.

title: Candle safety and burn tips
text:
# Candle safety and burn tips

## Wick trimming and maintenance

Before each burn, trim the wick to 1/4 inch. A wick that is too long produces soot, an unstable flame, and black smoke. Use scissors or a dedicated wick trimmer, and discard any charred debris that falls into the wax.

If the flame grows taller than 1 inch or flickers excessively, extinguish the candle, let it cool, trim the wick, and relight. A steady flame about 1/2 to 3/4 inch tall indicates proper burn conditions.

title: Candle safety and burn tips
text:
# Candle safety and burn tips

## Safe burning environment

Place the candle on a heat-resistant, stable surface away from drafts, vents, and flammable materials. Keep it out of reach of children and pets. Never leave a burning candle unattended, and extinguish it before leaving the room or going to sleep.

Stop burning when 1/2 inch of wax remains at the bottom. Continuing past this point can overheat the container and create a fire hazard.

title: Candle safety and burn tips
section_heading: Why the Holiday Candle is final sale
text:
# Candle safety and burn tips

## Why the Holiday Candle is final sale

Candles are consumable products with a limited shelf life, and scent performance can degrade if stored improperly or for extended periods. For this reason, our Holiday Candle is marked final sale and cannot be returned once purchased. See our guide on final-sale items for the complete policy.

title: Candle safety and burn tips
text:
# Candle safety and burn tips

## FAQ

**Can I burn the candle in a small bathroom or bedroom?**  
Yes, as long as the room is ventilated and the candle sits on a stable, heat-resistant surface. Avoid placing it near shower steam or directly under air vents.

**What if the candle develops an uneven surface after a few burns?**  
This is normal. As long as the first burn created a full melt pool, minor surface irregularities will not affect performance. Trim the wick and continue burning as usual.

**How do I clean soot from the container?**  
Let the candle cool completely, then wipe the interior glass with a damp cloth. Persistent soot indicates the wick was too long — trim it before the next burn.

title: Cast iron: seasoning and daily care
text:
# Cast iron: seasoning and daily care

Your Cast Iron Skillet, 10" (FG-KIT-001) ships with a factory seasoning layer already baked in, so you can cook right away. This guide covers that first cook, everyday cleaning, and how to bring the surface back if you spot rust or uneven patches. Cast iron builds character with use — a few simple habits keep it cooking beautifully for decades.

title: Cast iron: seasoning and daily care
section_heading: What "pre-seasoned" means and your first cook
text:
# Cast iron: seasoning and daily care

## What "pre-seasoned" means and your first cook

The skillet leaves our warehouse with three coats of vegetable oil baked at high heat. That initial seasoning prevents rust during shipping and gives you a head start, but it is thin. Your first few cooks will feel slightly sticky or show some light residue on the towel when you wipe the pan — that is normal.

Before the first use, rinse the skillet under warm water and dry it completely. Rub a very thin layer of neutral oil (canola, grapeseed, or vegetable) over the cooking surface with a paper towel, leaving almost no visible sheen. Heat the skillet over medium for two minutes, let it cool, then wipe out any excess. Now cook something with a bit of fat — eggs, bacon, or sautéed vegetables work well. The seasoning will darken and smooth out over the next several meals.

title: Cast iron: seasoning and daily care
section_heading: Everyday cleaning: soap is fine in moderation
text:
# Cast iron: seasoning and daily care

## Everyday cleaning: soap is fine in moderation

Rinse the skillet under hot water as soon as it is cool enough to handle. For stuck-on bits, use a stiff brush or a chain-mail scrubber and a small amount of mild dish soap. Modern dish soap will not strip seasoning the way lye-based soaps did generations ago, but you do not need much — a pea-sized drop is plenty.

Dry the pan immediately with a towel, then set it on low heat for one minute to evaporate any moisture in the pores. While it is still warm, wipe the interior with a lightly oiled paper towel. Store the skillet in a dry spot. If you stack cookware, place a paper towel between pieces to let air circulate.

Never soak cast iron overnight or run it through the dishwasher. For comparison, our Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set (FG-KIT-003) is dishwasher-safe — see our guide on Ceramic and dishwasher use for care details on that line.

title: Cast iron: seasoning and daily care
section_heading: How to remove rust and re-season
text:
# Cast iron: seasoning and daily care

## How to remove rust and re-season

Surface rust appears as orange or brown spots, usually after the pan sat wet or in humid storage. Light rust comes off with a few minutes of scrubbing.

Sprinkle coarse salt over the rusted area and scrub with half a potato or a damp cloth, applying firm pressure. Rinse, then dry the skillet completely over low heat. If rust remains, use fine steel wool or a scouring pad, working in small circles. Once the surface is smooth and rust-free, apply a thin coat of oil to the entire interior and bake the skillet upside down at 450°F for one hour, with foil on the rack below to catch drips. Let it cool in the oven. Repeat this baking step two or three times if the seasoning looks patchy. The pan will darken with each cycle.

Heavy rust that pits the metal cannot be fully removed at home, but the skillet remains safe to cook with once you rebuild the seasoning layer on top.

title: Cast iron: seasoning and daily care
text:
# Cast iron: seasoning and daily care

## FAQ

**Can I cook acidic foods like tomato sauce in cast iron?**  
Yes, but limit simmering time to under 30 minutes for the first few months. Acidic ingredients can lift seasoning if the layer is still thin. Once the pan is well-seasoned — dark, glossy, and non-stick — tomato-based dishes cause no harm.

**The surface looks blotchy after cleaning. Did I damage it?**  
Uneven color is cosmetic. Seasoning builds in layers, and some areas polymerize faster depending on heat exposure and oil distribution. Keep cooking with a bit of fat, and the finish will even out. See our cast-iron capacity guide for recipe ideas that build seasoning naturally.

**How often should I re-season the whole pan?**  
Most cooks never need to. If food starts sticking in multiple spots or the surface feels tacky after cleaning, a full oven re-season (described above) resets the layer. Otherwise, the thin oil wipe after each use maintains the finish indefinitely.

title: Ceramic and dishwasher use
text:
# Ceramic and dishwasher use

Fieldstone ceramic pieces—including our Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set and Espresso Cups—are dishwasher-safe and built for daily use. The glaze finish resists staining and holds up to standard dishwasher cycles. The key care point is avoiding thermal shock: never move ceramic directly from the refrigerator into a hot oven or from a hot oven into cold water.

title: Ceramic and dishwasher use
text:
# Ceramic and dishwasher use

## Dishwasher-safe ceramic items

All glazed ceramic products in the Fieldstone catalog can go in the dishwasher. This includes the Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set (FG-KIT-003) and the Espresso Cups, Set of 4 (FG-KIT-004). Use the normal wash cycle. Avoid the high-heat drying setting if your pieces have metallic trim, though standard Fieldstone ceramics have no metal accents.

Top-rack placement works well for smaller items like espresso cups. Larger mixing bowls fit securely on the bottom rack. Make sure bowls are angled so water drains rather than pooling inside.

Hand-washing with warm soapy water is always fine and may extend the life of any decorative elements. Either method keeps the glaze intact.

title: Ceramic and dishwasher use
text:
# Ceramic and dishwasher use

## Preventing thermal shock

Ceramic can crack when exposed to rapid temperature changes. Do not place a cold mixing bowl straight into a preheated oven, and do not run a hot ceramic piece under cold tap water.

If you store ingredients in a ceramic bowl in the refrigerator and plan to bake with them, let the bowl sit at room temperature for fifteen to twenty minutes first. When removing a hot bowl from the oven, set it on a wooden cutting board or trivet—never directly on a cold countertop or in the sink.

Room-temperature ceramic can go into a preheated oven without issue. Just avoid the extremes in either direction.

title: Ceramic and dishwasher use
text:
# Ceramic and dishwasher use

## Caring for the glaze

The glaze on Fieldstone ceramic resists most stains, but deep pigments from beets or turmeric may leave faint marks over time. A paste of baking soda and water, applied with a soft sponge, lifts most discoloration. Let the paste sit for five minutes before rinsing.

Avoid abrasive scouring pads or steel wool. These scratch the glaze and create tiny channels where stains can settle. A nylon scrub brush handles baked-on food without damaging the surface.

For general maintenance tips across our product lines, see our guide on cast iron: seasoning and daily care, which covers similar principles of heat management and surface protection.

title: Ceramic and dishwasher use
text:
# Ceramic and dishwasher use

## FAQ

**Can I microwave Fieldstone ceramic bowls?**  
Yes. Ceramic mixing bowls and espresso cups are microwave-safe as long as they have no metallic decoration. Heat liquids or food in short intervals and use oven mitts when removing hot items.

**What if a bowl develops a hairline crack?**  
Stop using it for food. A cracked bowl can harbor bacteria and may shatter under heat. Ceramic pieces with structural damage cannot be repaired safely.

**Do I need to season ceramic like cast iron?**  
No. Glazed ceramic does not require seasoning. Wash it before first use and you are ready to cook. The glaze provides a naturally non-stick surface that stays intact with normal cleaning.

title: Cutting board maintenance
text:
# Cutting board maintenance

Your walnut cutting board (FG-KIT-002) will last for years when you clean it correctly after each use and treat it with mineral oil once a month. Two mistakes crack hardwood faster than anything else: running it through the dishwasher and leaving it submerged in water. Follow the steps below to keep the board flat, smooth, and safe for food prep.

title: Cutting board maintenance
text:
# Cutting board maintenance

## Daily cleaning after use

Wash the board by hand with warm water and dish soap immediately after prep work. Use a sponge or soft brush to remove food particles from the grain. Rinse thoroughly under running water, then stand the board upright against a dish rack or lean it against the wall so air reaches both sides. The board should dry completely within two to three hours.

Never place the walnut board in the dishwasher. High heat and prolonged moisture exposure cause warping and deep cracks that cannot be repaired. Do not leave the board sitting in a sink full of water or submerged in any basin — even a few minutes of soaking weakens the wood fibers and pulls out the protective oil.

title: Cutting board maintenance
text:
# Cutting board maintenance

## Monthly mineral oil treatment

Once a month, apply food-grade mineral oil to restore moisture and prevent the wood from drying out. Pour about one tablespoon of oil onto the clean, dry board and spread it evenly across the surface with a lint-free cloth or paper towel. Work the oil into the grain using circular motions, then flip the board and coat the other side. Let the oil soak in for four to six hours or overnight.

Wipe away any excess oil that has not absorbed into the wood. The board is ready to use as soon as the surface feels dry to the touch. You can find food-grade mineral oil at most grocery or hardware stores. See our guide on long-term care for oiled wood for seasonal deep-conditioning steps.

title: Cutting board maintenance
text:
# Cutting board maintenance

## What to avoid

Two actions will damage your board beyond repair. First, never run it through the dishwasher — the combination of high water temperature, harsh detergent, and the drying cycle will crack the wood within a few wash cycles. Second, do not soak the board in water for any length of time. Even a brief soak draws moisture into the core and causes the wood to swell unevenly, leading to splits along the grain.

Avoid cutting directly on the board with serrated knives or cleavers, which gouge the surface and create grooves where bacteria can collect. Use the board for slicing vegetables, bread, and cheese. For raw meat, consider a separate plastic cutting board that you can sanitize at higher temperatures.

title: Cutting board maintenance
text:
# Cutting board maintenance

## FAQ

**How do I know when the board needs more oil?** 
If the surface looks dull or feels dry when you run your hand across it, apply mineral oil. A well-oiled board has a subtle sheen and feels slightly waxy, not rough or thirsty.

**Can I use olive oil or coconut oil instead of mineral oil?** 
No. Cooking oils turn rancid over time and leave a sticky residue that attracts dust. Food-grade mineral oil does not spoil and will not affect the smell or taste of food.

**The board has a small crack along one edge — can I still use it?** 
Small surface cracks are cosmetic and safe for prep work. If the crack runs through the full thickness of the board or feels unstable when you press on both sides, retire the board and contact us about a replacement.

title: Washing and drying linen
text:
# Washing and drying linen

Linen softens with every wash and gets better over time. Wash your Linen Throw Blanket in cold or warm water with mild detergent, tumble dry on low to speed softening, and embrace the natural texture — no ironing required.

title: Washing and drying linen
text:
# Washing and drying linen

## First wash expectations

Linen feels crisp when new. The first wash unlocks its signature soft drape. Wash your blanket before first use in cold or warm water (up to 105°F) on a gentle cycle with a small amount of mild detergent. Avoid bleach and fabric softener, which coat the fibers and prevent natural softening.

You will notice the fabric relax and gain softness immediately. After three to five washes, linen reaches its full soft hand. This evolution is part of the material's appeal. See our guide on About our linen for more on how European flax behaves over years of use.

title: Washing and drying linen
text:
# Washing and drying linen

## Water temperature and drying method

Cold water (under 80°F) preserves fiber strength and minimizes shrinkage. Warm water (80–105°F) cleans effectively and accelerates softening. Hot water is not necessary and may cause slight shrinkage over time.

Tumble drying on low heat softens linen faster than air drying. The mechanical action in the dryer breaks down pectin between fibers, giving you that lived-in feel in fewer washes. Remove the blanket while still slightly damp to reduce wrinkles. Low heat is essential — high heat weakens flax fibers and shortens the blanket's lifespan.

Air drying takes longer but extends fiber life. Hang or lay flat in a shaded area. Linen dries quickly due to its hollow fiber structure. Air-dried linen will soften more gradually, reaching full drape after more washes than tumble-dried pieces.

title: Washing and drying linen
text:
# Washing and drying linen

## Handling wrinkles without ironing

Linen wrinkles naturally. This texture is intentional and part of the fabric's relaxed aesthetic. If you prefer a smoother look, remove the blanket from the dryer while damp and shake it out firmly. Fold or drape it immediately. Residual moisture releases most creases as the fabric dries.

For stubborn wrinkles, hang the blanket in a steamy bathroom for ten minutes or mist lightly with water and smooth by hand. Linen responds to moisture and weight. You do not need an iron. Over time, regular use and washing train the fabric to hold its shape with minimal creasing.

title: Washing and drying linen
text:
# Washing and drying linen

## Stain treatment and long-term care

Treat stains promptly by blotting (not rubbing) with cold water and a small amount of mild detergent. Work from the outside of the stain inward. Rinse thoroughly before washing. Linen is durable and stain-resistant when addressed quickly.

Wash your blanket every two to four weeks with regular use, or as needed. Store clean and completely dry in a breathable cotton bag or on an open shelf. Avoid plastic bins, which trap moisture and can cause mildew. Linen's antimicrobial properties make it naturally resistant to odor between washes.

For guidance on caring for other natural fibers, see our article on Caring for wool rugs.

title: Washing and drying linen
text:
# Washing and drying linen

## FAQ

**Can I wash linen with other fabrics?** Yes. Wash linen with similar colors and weights. Avoid washing with heavy items like denim that can snag the weave or with synthetics that pill and transfer lint.

**How much will my linen blanket shrink?** Expect 2–3% shrinkage after the first wash if you use warm water and tumble dry. Cold water and air drying minimize this. Shrinkage stabilizes after the first few washes.

**Does linen pill or fray over time?** Quality linen does not pill. Edges may soften slightly with use, which is normal. If you notice loose threads, trim them with scissors rather than pulling.

title: Long-term care for oiled wood
text:
# Long-term care for oiled wood

Oiled walnut and oak develop character over time. A deepened patina, subtle color shifts, and fine scratches are natural aging, not damage. Monthly conditioning with food-safe mineral oil keeps the wood hydrated and protected. This guide walks you through a two-minute routine and shows you how to spot-repair deeper scratches so your pieces last decades.

title: Long-term care for oiled wood
text:
# Long-term care for oiled wood

## What normal aging looks like

Oiled wood changes color as it oxidizes. Walnut darkens in its first year, then gradually lightens to a warm chocolate-brown. Oak starts pale and can take on amber or gray tones depending on exposure to light. Both species develop small surface scratches from everyday use. These are expected and add texture, not structural weakness.

The finish itself wears thin in high-contact zones. On cutting boards, the center shows this first. On serving trays, handles fade faster. When the wood starts to look dry or pale, it needs oil. A monthly wipe-down prevents the finish from thinning unevenly.

Water rings and light stains lift with gentle sanding. If a white ring appears after a wet glass sits too long, let the wood dry completely, then rub the spot with 220-grit sandpaper in the direction of the grain. Wipe away dust and apply oil. The ring disappears as the wood absorbs moisture again.

title: Long-term care for oiled wood
text:
# Long-term care for oiled wood

## Two-minute monthly oil routine

Use food-grade mineral oil or a blend labeled for cutting boards. Avoid vegetable oils, which turn rancid. Pour a quarter-sized amount onto a clean cloth and rub it across the entire surface in long strokes following the grain. Let the oil sit for five minutes, then wipe away any excess with a dry cloth. The wood should look slightly darker but not wet.

Oil all sides of a board or tray, not just the top. Wood absorbs and releases moisture through every surface. Uneven oiling causes warping. If you store pieces stacked, separate them with parchment paper so oil does not transfer.

For pieces used daily, like our Walnut Cutting Board (FG-KIT-002), oil every two to three weeks in the first six months. After that, monthly is enough. For decorative items, quarterly oiling works fine. See our guide on cutting board maintenance for specifics on high-use care.

title: Long-term care for oiled wood
text:
# Long-term care for oiled wood

## Spot-repairing deeper scratches

Scratches that catch your fingernail need attention before they collect moisture and bacteria. Sand the scratch with 220-grit sandpaper, moving only in the direction of the grain. Sand a slightly larger area than the scratch itself so the repair blends. Wipe the dust away with a damp cloth and let the wood dry for ten minutes.

Apply mineral oil to the sanded area and let it soak for fifteen minutes. Wipe away the excess. The scratch should be less visible. If it still stands out, repeat the process with finer 320-grit sandpaper and oil again.

Deep gouges that expose raw wood may need professional refinishing. If a scratch goes through the oiled layer and the bare wood feels rough or fibrous, the structural integrity is still fine, but refinishing restores the protective seal.

title: Long-term care for oiled wood
text:
# Long-term care for oiled wood

## When to stop and replace

Wood splits along the grain when it dries out completely or experiences repeated moisture cycles. A single hairline crack does not mean the piece is unsafe, but cracks that widen or run the full length signal the end of usable life. Do not oil cracked wood heavily; excess moisture makes the split worse.

Warping happens when one side of a board stays wet while the other dries. If a cutting board rocks on the counter, flip your drying method. Lean boards upright against a wall instead of laying them flat. Warped pieces cannot be flattened at home.

Discoloration from mold or persistent odor means bacteria have colonized the wood grain. This happens when cutting boards stay damp in storage. If scrubbing with coarse salt and lemon does not remove the smell, replace the piece.

title: Long-term care for oiled wood
text:
# Long-term care for oiled wood

## FAQ

**How long does oiled wood last with monthly care?**  
Walnut and oak cutting boards last ten to twenty years with consistent oiling and proper drying. Serving pieces and trays, which see less moisture, often outlast that. The species matters less than the maintenance routine.

**Can I use olive oil or coconut oil instead of mineral oil?**  
No. Edible oils spoil and leave a sticky residue that traps dust and bacteria. Mineral oil is odorless, tasteless, and stays stable indefinitely. Beeswax blends are fine as a secondary layer for added water resistance.

**What if my board is already dried out and pale?**  
Sand the surface lightly with 220-grit sandpaper to open the grain, then oil it twice in one day. Let the first coat soak for thirty minutes before wiping and reapplying. The wood will darken as it rehydrates. Monthly oiling from that point keeps it conditioned.

title: Caring for wool rugs
text:
# Caring for wool rugs

Your Wool Area Rug, 5×7 (FG-HOM-002) stays soft and vibrant with regular light maintenance and prompt attention to spills. Weekly vacuuming and immediate blotting keep most wear at bay, while a professional cleaning every 12 to 18 months protects the fibers for years of use.

title: Caring for wool rugs
text:
# Caring for wool rugs

## Weekly maintenance

Vacuum your wool rug once or twice a week using a canister vacuum or an upright with the beater bar turned off. The beater bar can snag or fray wool fibers, especially along the edges. If your vacuum lacks a beater-bar shutoff, use the hose attachment with a soft-bristle floor tool.

Rotate the rug 180 degrees every three months so foot traffic wears the pile evenly. High-traffic areas develop a slight sheen over time—rotation distributes that patina across the surface.

Use a rug pad underneath to prevent slipping and to cushion the backing. The pad also allows air to circulate, which reduces moisture buildup and extends the rug's life.

title: Caring for wool rugs
text:
# Caring for wool rugs

## Treating spills and spots

Blot liquid spills immediately with a clean white cloth or paper towel. Press down firmly and lift—do not rub, which pushes the liquid deeper into the fibers. Work from the outer edge of the spill toward the center to keep it from spreading.

For water-based spills like coffee or juice, blot until no more liquid transfers to the cloth. If a stain remains, mix one teaspoon of mild dish soap with one cup of lukewarm water. Dampen a cloth with the solution, blot the stain gently, then blot again with a cloth dampened in plain water to rinse. Let the area air-dry completely.

For grease or oil, sprinkle cornstarch or baking soda over the spot and let it sit for 15 minutes to absorb the oil. Vacuum up the powder, then treat any residue with the soap solution described above.

Avoid enzyme cleaners, bleach, or steam cleaners. Wool is a protein fiber, and harsh chemicals or high heat can cause permanent damage or discoloration.

title: Caring for wool rugs
text:
# Caring for wool rugs

## Professional cleaning

Plan to have your rug professionally cleaned every 12 to 18 months, depending on household traffic and whether you have pets. Professional cleaners use pH-balanced solutions and controlled drying that protect wool's natural lanolin and prevent shrinkage.

Between cleanings, if a stain resists home treatment or if the rug develops an odor, contact a cleaner sooner. Waiting too long allows stains to set and odors to bond with the fibers.

Our wool is sourced from certified farms that prioritize animal welfare and sustainable grazing. See our guide on wool sourcing and certifications for details on how those practices influence the rug's durability and texture.

title: Caring for wool rugs
text:
# Caring for wool rugs

## FAQ

**Can I wash my wool rug at home?**  
No. Wool rugs require controlled moisture and drying to avoid felting, shrinking, or color bleeding. Home washing machines and even bathtub soaking introduce too much water and agitation. Stick to spot cleaning and professional service.

**How do I remove pet hair from the rug?**  
Vacuum with the beater bar off, then use a rubber-bristle brush or a damp rubber glove to lift stubborn hair from the pile. The static from rubber grabs hair that suction alone misses.

**Will sunlight fade my rug?**  
Direct sunlight can fade natural dyes over time. Rotate the rug regularly and use window treatments during peak sun hours if the rug sits in a bright room. Fading happens gradually and evenly with rotation, so it remains less noticeable.

title: Damaged or defective items
text:
# Damaged or defective items

If an item arrives damaged or has a manufacturing defect, we will replace it or issue a full refund at no cost to you. Damage claims are handled separately from standard returns—you will not pay a restocking fee, and the 30-day return window does not strictly apply when something arrives broken or flawed.

title: Damaged or defective items
section_heading: What qualifies as damaged or defective
text:
# Damaged or defective items

## What qualifies as damaged or defective

Damage includes items that arrive broken, cracked, chipped, torn, or visibly crushed during shipping. Defects are manufacturing flaws: a skillet with an uneven cooking surface, a blanket with visible stitching gaps, or a mug with glaze defects that affect use.

Cosmetic imperfections that do not affect function—a minor scuff on the underside of a cutting board, slight color variation in natural materials—fall outside the defect policy. If you are unsure whether an issue qualifies, submit photos and our support team will review.

title: Damaged or defective items
text:
# Damaged or defective items

## Starting a damage claim

Reply to your order confirmation email with the subject line "Damage claim — [your order number]" and include two or three clear photos showing the issue. A wide shot of the full item and a close-up of the damage help us assess quickly.

You do not need to return the item before we respond. In many cases we can approve a replacement or refund based on photos alone, especially for heavy or fragile items where return shipping adds unnecessary cost and handling.

Your order number follows the format FG-NNNNNN and appears in your confirmation email. If you cannot locate it, include the email address used at checkout and an approximate order date.

title: Damaged or defective items
text:
# Damaged or defective items

## What happens next

Our team typically reviews damage claims within one business day. If the claim is approved, you will receive a prepaid return label by email. Once the warehouse confirms receipt, a replacement ships or a full refund posts to your original payment method within 5–7 business days.

If a damaged item was marked final sale, the damage policy still applies—final-sale restrictions cover buyer's remorse, not items that arrive broken. See our guide on **Final sale items** for more on what those restrictions normally cover.

For items where return shipping is impractical (a heavy rug with significant damage, for instance), we may approve a refund without requiring return. That decision is made case by case.

title: Damaged or defective items
text:
# Damaged or defective items

## Tips for a faster resolution

Clear photos make the difference. Show the product label or SKU tag if possible, and photograph the outer shipping box if it arrived visibly crushed. Natural light works better than overhead indoor lighting.

If you are reporting a defect rather than visible damage, describe what is wrong in one or two sentences: "The skillet wobbles on a flat surface" or "The blanket has a 3-inch tear along the hem." Specifics help us route the claim correctly.

Standard returns have a $7.95 restocking fee for opened items, but damage claims do not. If you are unsure whether to file a damage claim or a standard return, visit **How to start a return** for a comparison. You can also reach our support team through the **Contacting customer support** article if you need help deciding.

title: Damaged or defective items
text:
# Damaged or defective items

## FAQ

**Do I need to keep the original packaging?**  
Not required, but keeping the box for a few days after delivery makes it easier to document shipping damage if you discover it later.

**What if I notice a defect two months after delivery?**  
Contact us. Manufacturing defects reported within a reasonable window are usually covered even past 30 days, though we will ask for photos and a description of the issue.

**Can I get a replacement if the item is out of stock?**  
If a replacement is not available, we will issue a full refund. You can choose store credit if you prefer, but we cannot hold inventory or backorder replacements for damage claims.

title: Exchanges vs. returns
text:
# Exchanges vs. returns

Fieldstone processes all exchanges as a return followed by a new order. We do not hold or swap items in place. This means you'll receive a refund for your original purchase, then place a separate order for the replacement item. While this takes a few more days than a direct swap, it gives you full control over what you order next and ensures accurate inventory.

title: Exchanges vs. returns
section_heading: Why we process exchanges as two separate transactions
text:
# Exchanges vs. returns

## Why we process exchanges as two separate transactions

An exchange at Fieldstone works in two steps. First, you return the item you no longer want using our standard return process. Once our warehouse receives and inspects it, we issue a refund to your original payment method. Second, you place a new order for the item you'd prefer—different size, color, or product entirely.

We handle exchanges this way because our inventory updates in real time. Holding an item during a return creates timing issues: the replacement might sell out, or your return might not meet our condition guidelines. Processing each step separately ensures you see current stock, current pricing, and clear refund timelines. See our guide on refund timelines for details on when your refund will land.

title: Exchanges vs. returns
text:
# Exchanges vs. returns

## How to exchange an item

Start your return the same way you would for any unwanted item. Visit the returns page, enter your email and order number (format FG-NNNNNN), and select the item to return. Print the prepaid label and ship the package within five days.

Opened items in original condition carry a $7.95 return shipping fee, deducted from your refund. Unopened items return free. Final-sale items marked in the catalog—currently FG-SAL-001 and FG-SAL-002—cannot be returned or exchanged.

Once your return reaches our warehouse, expect your refund within 5–7 business days. After the refund posts, place a new order for the replacement. Standard shipping takes 3–5 business days; expedited shipping takes 1–2 business days.

title: Exchanges vs. returns
section_heading: What this means for timing and inventory
text:
# Exchanges vs. returns

## What this means for timing and inventory

Because an exchange is two transactions, the full process takes longer than an in-place swap. Budget roughly two weeks from the day you ship your return to the day your replacement arrives, assuming standard shipping both ways.

If the item you want is low in stock, place your new order as soon as you ship the return—you do not need to wait for the refund to post before ordering. Just know that you'll pay for the new order upfront. The refund will arrive on your original payment method a few days later.

Items can sell out between your return and your new order. Check the product page before you return to confirm the replacement is in stock. We cannot reserve inventory during a return window.

title: Exchanges vs. returns
text:
# Exchanges vs. returns

## FAQ

**Can I exchange a final-sale item?**  
No. Items marked final sale in the catalog cannot be returned or exchanged. This applies to FG-SAL-001 (Holiday Candle) and FG-SAL-002 (Seasonal Tea Towels, Set of 2).

**Will I pay shipping twice?**  
You'll pay return shipping if your item was opened ($7.95, deducted from your refund). Your new order will have its own shipping charge—standard or expedited, depending on what you choose at checkout. Unopened returns ship free.

**What if the new item costs more or less than the original?**  
You'll receive a full refund for the returned item (minus any return shipping fee), then pay the current price for the new order. If prices have changed between orders, you'll see the difference reflected in the two transactions.

title: Final sale items
text:
# Final sale items

Final-sale items cannot be returned or exchanged once your order ships. These are typically seasonal or limited-run products offered at a discount, and the final-sale designation appears clearly on the product page before you add the item to your cart. If a final-sale item arrives damaged or defective, we will replace it or issue a refund.

title: Final sale items
text:
# Final sale items

## Which items are final sale

Final-sale products are marked with a "Final Sale" badge on the product page, directly below the price. Currently, our final-sale catalog includes:

- **FG-SAL-001** — Holiday Candle — $18.00
- **FG-SAL-002** — Seasonal Tea Towels, Set of 2 — $22.00

The final-sale list changes with the season. Before completing your purchase, double-check the product page and your cart summary to confirm which items carry the final-sale restriction.

title: Final sale items
section_heading: Why some items are final sale
text:
# Final sale items

## Why some items are final sale

We use final-sale pricing for seasonal goods, limited editions, and clearance inventory. These products move quickly, and the discounted price reflects the no-return policy. Final-sale items help us offer you great value on curated pieces while managing inventory turns responsibly.

title: Final sale items
section_heading: What to do if a final-sale item arrives damaged
text:
# Final sale items

## What to do if a final-sale item arrives damaged

Final sale means no returns for change of mind, but it does not mean you are stuck with a damaged product. If your final-sale item arrives broken, defective, or visibly damaged in transit, contact Ember with your order number and a photo of the issue. We will send a replacement if stock permits, or issue a full refund if the item is no longer available. See our guide on damaged or defective items for details on how to document and report the problem.

Standard return eligibility and the 30-day window do not apply to final-sale items, but our quality guarantee does. You should receive what you ordered in good condition, regardless of the sale terms.

title: Final sale items
text:
# Final sale items

## FAQ

**Can I exchange a final-sale item for a different size or color?**  
No. Final-sale items cannot be returned or exchanged for any reason except damage or defect.

**Will I know before I buy that something is final sale?**  
Yes. The product page displays a "Final Sale" badge below the price, and your cart summary repeats the designation before checkout.

**What if I order a final-sale item by mistake?**  
Once the order ships, the final-sale policy takes effect. If you realize the mistake before shipment, contact Ember immediately—we may be able to cancel the order, though we cannot guarantee it.

title: How to start a return
text:
# How to start a return

Starting a return takes about five minutes. You'll need your order number and email address, then we'll send you a prepaid return label and instructions. Once your item arrives at our warehouse, your refund processes within 5–7 business days.

title: How to start a return
section_heading: What you need before you start
text:
# How to start a return

## What you need before you start

Have your order number handy. It starts with FG- followed by six digits (for example, FG-123456) and appears in your order confirmation email. You'll also need the email address you used at checkout.

If your item is still within the 30-day return window and isn't marked final sale, you're all set. See our guide on return eligibility and the 30-day window for details on what qualifies.

title: How to start a return
text:
# How to start a return

## Request your return authorization

Visit the returns page and enter your order number and email address. Select the item you want to return and choose a reason from the dropdown menu. This helps us improve, but it does not affect your eligibility.

You'll receive a return authorization email within a few minutes. That email contains your RMA number and a prepaid shipping label as a PDF attachment. Print the label or display it on your phone at the carrier location.

title: How to start a return
text:
# How to start a return

## Pack and ship your item

Place the item in its original packaging if you still have it. If not, use a sturdy box that protects the item during transit. Attach the prepaid label to the outside of the package, covering or removing any old shipping labels.

Drop off your package at any location that accepts the carrier shown on your label. You do not need to schedule a pickup. Keep your drop-off receipt until you see the refund land in your account.

Unopened items ship back for free. If you opened the item but it remains in original condition, a $7.95 return shipping fee applies and will be deducted from your refund. For more on how fees work, see return shipping fees.

title: How to start a return
section_heading: What happens after the warehouse receives your return
text:
# How to start a return

## What happens after the warehouse receives your return

Our team inspects the item within two business days of arrival. If everything checks out, your refund processes to the original payment method. Most refunds appear within 5–7 business days, though your bank may take an additional day or two to post the credit.

You'll receive an email confirmation once the refund processes. If there's an issue with the return—damage during shipping or missing components—we'll contact you at the email address on file.

title: How to start a return
text:
# How to start a return

## FAQ

**What if I lost my order number?** Check your email inbox for "Fieldstone Goods order confirmation" or contact Ember, our support agent, through the chat widget. She can look up your order using your email and shipping address.

**Can I return part of a multi-item order?** Yes. During the return request, select only the items you want to send back. The rest of your order is unaffected.

**Do I need to include the original receipt?** No. Your RMA number ties the return to your order, so you do not need to include a packing slip or printed receipt.

title: Refund timelines
text:
# Refund timelines

Your refund will land on your original payment method within 5–7 business days after our warehouse receives and processes your return. This window accounts for bank processing time and varies by card issuer. The clock starts when the warehouse confirms receipt, not when you print the return label or drop off the package.

title: Refund timelines
text:
# Refund timelines

## When the refund timer begins

The 5–7 business day window begins the moment our warehouse scans your returned item into inventory and confirms the condition matches our return eligibility standards. Shipping time does not count toward this period. If you print a return label on Monday and the carrier delivers the package to our warehouse on Friday, the refund timer starts Friday.

You will receive an email confirming warehouse receipt. That confirmation marks the beginning of the 5–7 business day refund window. If you started your return through our returns portal and provided your order number (format FG-NNNNNN) and email address, the confirmation goes to that address automatically.

title: Refund timelines
section_heading: Why 5–7 business days instead of instant
text:
# Refund timelines

## Why 5–7 business days instead of instant

Once our warehouse processes your return, we issue the refund immediately. The 5–7 business day range reflects how long banks and card networks take to post credits. Credit cards typically post refunds within five business days. Debit cards sometimes take the full seven. Weekends and bank holidays extend the timeline, since those are not business days.

We cannot expedite this period. The delay occurs between our payment processor and your bank. If seven business days have passed since the warehouse receipt email and the refund has not posted, contact your card issuer first. They can confirm whether the credit is pending on their end.

title: Refund timelines
text:
# Refund timelines

## Checking refund status

Your warehouse receipt email includes the refund amount and the date we released the funds. If you need to verify whether the warehouse received your return, check the tracking number on your return label. Delivered status means the package arrived; warehouse receipt occurs within one business day of delivery in most cases.

For returns with a $7.95 return shipping fee (opened items in original condition), the refunded amount will be your original order total minus that fee. Final-sale items cannot be returned, so no refund is possible for those SKUs. See our guide on return eligibility and the 30-day window for details on which items qualify.

title: Refund timelines
section_heading: What happens if the timeline exceeds seven days
text:
# Refund timelines

## What happens if the timeline exceeds seven days

If eight or more business days have passed since your warehouse receipt email, and your bank confirms no pending credit, reach out through our support chat. The agent will verify the refund was issued and, if needed, escalate to a billing specialist. Have your order number and the warehouse receipt email ready.

Payment method changes also affect timing. If the original card is closed or expired, the issuing bank may return the funds to us. In that case, a specialist will coordinate an alternative refund method. This adds time but ensures you receive the full amount.

title: Refund timelines
text:
# Refund timelines

## FAQ

**Can I get a refund before you receive the return?**  
No. The warehouse must confirm receipt and inspect the item before issuing a refund. This protects against cases where a package is lost in transit or arrives in a condition that does not meet return guidelines.

**Will the refund show as pending before it posts?**  
Sometimes. Some card issuers display pending credits; others do not show the transaction until it fully posts. Check with your bank if you want visibility into pending activity.

**Does the 5–7 day window apply to all payment methods?**  
Yes. Credit cards, debit cards, and digital wallets tied to a card all follow the same 5–7 business day standard once we release the refund.

title: Return eligibility and the 30-day window
text:
# Return eligibility and the 30-day window

You have 30 days from the delivery date to return most items. The clock starts when the package arrives at your address, not when you place the order. Unopened items return for free; opened items in original condition have a $7.95 return shipping fee. Three categories are never eligible: final-sale items, items outside the 30-day window, and items that are worn or damaged beyond their original condition.

title: Return eligibility and the 30-day window
section_heading: When the 30-day window starts and ends
text:
# Return eligibility and the 30-day window

## When the 30-day window starts and ends

The return window begins on the delivery date shown in your shipping confirmation email. If your order arrived on March 15, you have until April 14 to initiate the return. Orders placed earlier but delivered later still follow the delivery date, so a week in transit does not shorten your window.

You must start the return process by the end of day 30. We use the timestamp when you submit the return request, not when the package arrives back at our warehouse. See our guide on How to start a return for step-by-step instructions.

title: Return eligibility and the 30-day window
section_heading: What counts as unopened versus opened
text:
# Return eligibility and the 30-day window

## What counts as unopened versus opened

Unopened means the product is still in its original packaging with all seals, tags, and protective wrapping intact. For items like the Cast Iron Skillet (FG-KIT-001) or the Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set (FG-KIT-003), this means the cardboard box is sealed and the product has not been removed.

Opened items are those you have removed from packaging, inspected, or tested. You can return opened items as long as they remain in original condition: no scratches, stains, chips, or missing pieces. The Walnut Cutting Board (FG-KIT-002) can be returned if you unboxed it, but not if it shows knife marks or water damage. Opened returns incur a $7.95 return shipping fee to cover our processing costs. Unopened returns ship free.

title: Return eligibility and the 30-day window
section_heading: Three categories that are never eligible
text:
# Return eligibility and the 30-day window

## Three categories that are never eligible

**Final-sale items** cannot be returned under any circumstance. These are marked as final sale at checkout and in your order confirmation. Examples include the Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001) and Seasonal Tea Towels (FG-SAL-002). For the full list and reasoning, see our guide on Final sale items.

**Items outside the 30-day window** cannot be processed as standard returns. If you discover an issue after day 30, contact us. We cannot promise an exception, but a specialist will review your situation.

**Items worn, used, or damaged** beyond their original condition are not eligible. This includes cookware with food residue, linens with stains or pet hair, and rugs with stretched backing. If your item arrived damaged or defective, that is a separate process—see our guide on Damaged or defective items for next steps.

title: Return eligibility and the 30-day window
text:
# Return eligibility and the 30-day window

## FAQ

**Does the 30-day window include weekends and holidays?**  
Yes. The window is 30 calendar days, not business days. If day 30 falls on a weekend, you still need to submit your return request by that date.

**What if I received my order as a gift?**  
The same 30-day window applies, starting from the delivery date to the recipient's address. The recipient can initiate the return using the order number from the packing slip. Refunds return to the original purchaser's payment method.

**Can I return one item from a multi-item order?**  
Yes. Each item in your order is eligible independently, as long as it meets the conditions above. You can keep the Linen Throw Blanket (FG-HOM-001) and return the Espresso Cups (FG-KIT-004) from the same order.

title: Return shipping fees
text:
# Return shipping fees

Returns are free for unopened items in original packaging. If you've opened an item but it's still in its original condition, we charge a $7.95 return shipping fee per order. This fee covers the cost of return logistics and helps us keep prices fair for all customers.

title: Return shipping fees
text:
# Return shipping fees

## When returns are free

You will not pay a return shipping fee if every item in your return is unopened and in its original packaging. The item must be unused, with all tags and protective materials intact. We provide a prepaid return label at no charge.

This applies to standard-return items only. Final-sale items like the Holiday Candle (FG-SAL-001) and Seasonal Tea Towels (FG-SAL-002) cannot be returned regardless of condition.

title: Return shipping fees
text:
# Return shipping fees

## The $7.95 opened-item fee

If you've opened an item but kept it in original condition—no damage, stains, or missing pieces—you can still return it within 30 days of delivery. We'll deduct $7.95 from your refund to cover return shipping costs.

The fee applies once per return order, not per item. If you're returning three opened products in a single return, you pay $7.95 total. See our guide on return eligibility and the 30-day window for details on condition requirements.

title: Return shipping fees
section_heading: Mixed returns: unopened and opened items
text:
# Return shipping fees

## Mixed returns: unopened and opened items
