If your return includes both unopened and opened items, the $7.95 fee applies to the entire return. We assess the fee based on whether any item in the return has been opened.

For example, if you're returning an unopened Walnut Cutting Board (FG-KIT-002) and an opened Cast Iron Skillet (FG-KIT-001), the $7.95 fee will be deducted from your total refund. To avoid the fee, you could submit two separate returns—one for the unopened board, one for the opened skillet—but most customers find it simpler to send everything together.

title: Return shipping fees
text:
# Return shipping fees

## How the fee is processed

When you start a return through our system, you'll see the fee displayed before you confirm. We deduct it from your refund after the warehouse receives and inspects your items. Refunds land on your original payment method within 5–7 business days of warehouse receipt.

If you have questions about whether your item qualifies as unopened, check the packaging when it arrives. Original shrink wrap, sealed boxes, and attached hang tags all indicate unopened status. For step-by-step instructions, see how to start a return.

title: Return shipping fees
text:
# Return shipping fees

## FAQ

**Can I avoid the fee by using my own shipping label?**  
No. The $7.95 fee applies to opened items regardless of how you ship them back. We provide a prepaid label for convenience, but using your own carrier will not waive the fee.

**Does the fee apply to defective or damaged items?**  
No. If an item arrives defective or damaged, return shipping is free and we will issue a full refund with no deduction. Contact Ember with your order number (format FG-NNNNNN) and photos of the issue to begin that process.

**What if I'm returning only final-sale items?**  
Final-sale items cannot be returned, so no return shipping fee applies. You cannot initiate a return for items marked final sale at the time of purchase.

title: Cast iron skillet capacity guide
text:
# Cast iron skillet capacity guide

The Cast Iron Skillet, 10" (FG-KIT-001) measures 10 inches across the top rim and holds approximately 2 quarts of liquid. It comfortably cooks 2–4 servings depending on the dish, weighs 5.2 pounds, and works on all stovetops and in ovens up to 500°F.

title: Cast iron skillet capacity guide
text:
# Cast iron skillet capacity guide

## Cooking capacity by servings

The 10-inch diameter gives you enough cooking surface for most everyday meals. A typical breakfast fits 3–4 eggs or 4–6 strips of bacon without crowding. For pan-seared proteins, you can cook two 6-ounce chicken breasts or a 12-ounce steak with room to flip comfortably. Shallow braises and skillet pastas work well at the 2-quart capacity, serving 3–4 people as a main dish.

If you are cooking for one or two, the skillet handles single portions with plenty of space. For larger gatherings, you may want to cook in batches or use multiple pans.

title: Cast iron skillet capacity guide
text:
# Cast iron skillet capacity guide

## Oven and stovetop compatibility

This skillet is oven-safe up to 500°F with no additional components to remove. The single-piece cast iron construction, including the handle, makes it a reliable choice for stovetop-to-oven recipes like cornbread, frittatas, or roasted chicken thighs. It works on gas, electric coil, glass-top, and induction cooktops. On induction, the skillet heats efficiently due to its magnetic cast iron material.

Preheat gradually on medium heat rather than high to avoid hot spots. Cast iron retains heat longer than nonstick, so you may find yourself lowering the burner setting once the pan comes to temperature.

title: Cast iron skillet capacity guide
text:
# Cast iron skillet capacity guide

## Weight and handling expectations

At 5.2 pounds, the Cast Iron Skillet, 10" feels substantially heavier than a comparable nonstick pan, which typically weighs 1.5 to 2 pounds. The extra weight comes from the thick cast iron walls that provide even heat distribution and durability. You will notice the difference immediately when lifting the skillet, especially when it is full of food.

The handle stays cool on the stovetop for short cooking tasks, but it heats up during longer cooking times and always gets hot in the oven. Use a dry towel or oven mitt when moving the skillet after the first few minutes of cooking.

title: Cast iron skillet capacity guide
text:
# Cast iron skillet capacity guide

## Maintenance overview

Cast iron requires a layer of polymerized oil called seasoning to prevent rust and create a natural nonstick surface. The skillet arrives pre-seasoned and ready to use. After cooking, wash it by hand with warm water and a small amount of soap if needed, then dry it completely and apply a thin coat of cooking oil. See our guide on cast iron seasoning and care for more detailed instructions on building and maintaining the seasoning layer over time.

Do not put the skillet in the dishwasher or leave it soaking in water, as both will strip the seasoning and promote rust.

title: Cast iron skillet capacity guide
text:
# Cast iron skillet capacity guide

## FAQ

**Can I use metal utensils in this skillet?** Yes. Cast iron is durable enough to handle metal spatulas, tongs, and spoons without scratching or damaging the cooking surface. Metal utensils will not harm the seasoning layer.

**Will the skillet work on my glass cooktop?** Yes, but lift it rather than sliding it across the surface to avoid scratching the glass. Cast iron's rough bottom texture can damage smooth cooktops if dragged.

**How do I know when the skillet is hot enough?** Flick a few drops of water onto the surface. If they sizzle and evaporate within 2–3 seconds, the skillet is ready for cooking. Cast iron takes longer to heat than nonstick but holds that heat consistently once it reaches temperature.

title: Cutting board dimensions and workflow
text:
# Cutting board dimensions and workflow

The Walnut Cutting Board (FG-KIT-002) measures 18 inches long by 12 inches wide by 1.5 inches thick. It's milled from a single slab of North American black walnut, giving you a stable work surface that fits standard countertops and pairs well with chef's knives from 6 to 10 inches. The single-slab construction means no glue lines and better structural integrity over time.

title: Cutting board dimensions and workflow
text:
# Cutting board dimensions and workflow

## Board footprint and counter fit

At 18" × 12", the board occupies about 1.5 square feet of counter space. That's large enough to handle prep work for two to four servings without crowding your knife hand, but compact enough to store upright in a cabinet or lean against a backsplash when not in use. The 1.5-inch thickness keeps the board stable during chopping and prevents warping from moisture exposure on one side.

Most home kitchens have at least 24 inches of continuous counter space near the sink or stove. The board leaves 6 inches of clearance on a standard 24-inch counter section, giving you room for a bowl or plate alongside your prep area. If your counter depth is standard (25 inches), the 12-inch width leaves 13 inches behind the board for small appliances or a utensil crock.

title: Cutting board dimensions and workflow
text:
# Cutting board dimensions and workflow

## Pairing with common knife sizes

The 18-inch length gives you a full stroke for slicing and rocking cuts with chef's knives up to 10 inches. A standard 8-inch chef's knife has plenty of runway for dicing onions or mincing herbs without your knuckles hitting the edge of the board. The 12-inch width accommodates crosswise cuts for larger vegetables like bell peppers or summer squash.

For smaller tasks, paring knives and utility knades (3 to 6 inches) work comfortably on one half of the board, leaving the other half clean for plating or gathering prepped ingredients. The walnut surface is dense enough to resist deep scoring from sharp blades but soft enough that it won't dull your edge as quickly as bamboo or plastic.

title: Cutting board dimensions and workflow
text:
# Cutting board dimensions and workflow

## Single-slab construction

This board is milled from one continuous piece of walnut, not edge-glued strips. Single-slab boards have no seams where moisture or food particles can collect, making them easier to clean and less prone to splitting along glue lines. The grain runs lengthwise, which adds strength and creates the distinctive dark-brown streaks walnut is known for.

Each board shows slight color variation because we source from individual trees, not factory-dyed stock. See our guide on where our wood comes from for details on how we select and dry the walnut before milling. The natural variation means your board is one of a kind, though dimensions and thickness remain consistent across every unit.

title: Cutting board dimensions and workflow
text:
# Cutting board dimensions and workflow

## Care and longevity

Walnut is a hardwood that holds up to daily use when maintained correctly. Hand-wash with warm water and mild soap, then dry immediately. Avoid soaking or running it through the dishwasher, which can cause cracking. Monthly oiling with food-safe mineral oil keeps the surface conditioned and prevents moisture from penetrating the grain. For detailed care instructions, see our guide on cutting board maintenance.

With proper care, the board develops a darker patina over the first year of use. Light knife marks are normal and blend into the grain over time. The 1.5-inch thickness gives you enough material to sand and refinish the surface if deep cuts accumulate, though most home cooks find that unnecessary for the board's usable life.

title: Espresso cup volumes and fit
text:
# Espresso cup volumes and fit

The Espresso Cups (FG-KIT-004) hold 2.5 fluid ounces to the rim and are designed for classic Italian-style espresso service. Each cup fits a double shot with room for crema, sits under most home espresso machines, and uses a demitasse profile rather than a latte or cappuccino shape. This guide covers fill capacity, machine clearance, and why these cups are sized the way they are.

title: Espresso cup volumes and fit
text:
# Espresso cup volumes and fit

## Volume and standard espresso pulls

Each cup holds 2.5 fl oz (74 ml) when filled to the rim. A standard single espresso pull is about 1 oz, and a double is 2 oz. That leaves roughly half an ounce of headroom for crema and foam. If you pull ristretto shots (shorter extractions around 0.75 oz per pull), the cups accommodate two singles with generous space. For lungo shots (3 oz or more), the cup will overflow — these are not built for Americano-style volumes.

The demitasse profile (narrow body, flared rim) concentrates aroma and keeps the crema intact longer than a wide-mouth cup. If you typically drink milk-based drinks like lattes or flat whites, these cups are too small; they are purpose-built for straight espresso.

title: Espresso cup volumes and fit
text:
# Espresso cup volumes and fit

## Clearance under home machines

The cups measure 2.25 inches tall (5.7 cm) and 2.5 inches wide at the rim. Most home espresso machines (Breville, Gaggia, Rancilio, De'Longhi models) provide 3 to 4 inches of clearance between the spout and drip tray. These cups fit comfortably under that range. If your machine has an adjustable drip tray, you may need to lower it for the cup to sit flush beneath the portafilter spouts.

Machines with very low group heads or fixed single-spout commercial-style outputs may require you to tilt the cup slightly during extraction. For ultra-compact pod machines with less than 2.5 inches of clearance, these cups will not fit.

title: Espresso cup volumes and fit
section_heading: Why demitasse and not latte cups
text:
# Espresso cup volumes and fit

## Why demitasse and not latte cups

Demitasse (French for "half cup") is the traditional vessel for espresso. The smaller volume keeps the liquid hot longer and concentrates the aromatics at the rim. Latte cups, by contrast, are 6 to 8 oz and designed for milk dilution. The Espresso Cups ship as a set of four, so you have enough for back-to-back pulls without washing between rounds.

The ceramic is safe for daily dishwasher use — see our guide on ceramic cookware and dishwasher use for care details. The glaze is rated for temperatures up to 450°F, so you can preheat the cups in a warming drawer or on top of the machine without risk of cracking.

title: Espresso cup volumes and fit
text:
# Espresso cup volumes and fit

## FAQ

**Can I use these for tea or cortado drinks?**  
Yes. The 2.5 oz capacity works for small tea servings or cortados (1:1 espresso and steamed milk). For cappuccinos, you would need a larger cup.

**Do the cups come with saucers?**  
No. The set includes four cups only. Saucers are sold separately and not currently stocked.

**Will these fit in a standard cup warmer tray?**  
Most electric cup warmers accommodate cups up to 3 inches in diameter. These cups measure 2.5 inches at the rim, so they fit standard warming trays without issue.

title: Mixing bowl set volumes
text:
# Mixing bowl set volumes

The Ceramic Mixing Bowl Set (FG-KIT-003) includes three nested bowls with capacities of 1.5 quarts, 3 quarts, and 5 quarts. The smallest works well for whisking a few eggs or tossing a side salad, the medium handles cake batter or marinating chicken, and the largest is built for bread dough, large salads, or serving a crowd.

title: Mixing bowl set volumes
text:
# Mixing bowl set volumes

## Bowl dimensions and capacities

Each bowl is wheel-thrown stoneware with a glazed interior and matte exterior.

**Small bowl:** 1.5-quart capacity, approximately 7 inches in diameter and 3.5 inches tall. Use this for whisking two to four eggs, mixing vinaigrettes, or holding prepped ingredients like chopped herbs or nuts.

**Medium bowl:** 3-quart capacity, approximately 9 inches in diameter and 4.5 inches tall. This size handles pancake or muffin batter (roughly 12 to 18 muffins), marinating up to two pounds of protein, or tossing a salad for four to six people.

**Large bowl:** 5-quart capacity, approximately 11 inches in diameter and 5.5 inches tall. Ideal for bread dough (two to three loaves), mixing cookie dough for several dozen cookies, or serving pasta or grain salads at gatherings. The wide rim gives you room to fold ingredients without spilling.

All three bowls nest inside one another for compact storage.

title: Mixing bowl set volumes
text:
# Mixing bowl set volumes

## Typical uses by bowl size

Match the bowl to the task to avoid overflows and make mixing easier.

The **1.5-quart bowl** is your prep bowl. Crack eggs, whisk a small amount of cream, hold grated cheese, or mix a single serving of overnight oats. It is too small for stand-mixer attachments but works well with a hand whisk or fork.

The **3-quart bowl** bridges everyday baking and cooking. Mix batter for a standard 9x13 cake, toss a pound of roasted vegetables with olive oil and seasoning, or marinate chicken thighs. This is the most versatile size in the set.

The **5-quart bowl** handles volume. Knead pizza or sandwich dough, prepare a large batch of mashed potatoes, mix fruit salad for a potluck, or use it as a serving bowl for popcorn or chips. The tall sides contain splashes when you are folding or beating by hand.

title: Mixing bowl set volumes
text:
# Mixing bowl set volumes

## Care and cleaning

All three bowls are dishwasher-safe on the top rack. Hand washing extends the life of the glaze and prevents chipping from rack contact. See our guide on ceramic cookware and dishwasher use for more details on protecting stoneware finishes.

Avoid thermal shock by letting hot bowls cool before rinsing with cold water. The stoneware can chip if dropped on hard surfaces, so handle the nested set carefully when unstacking.

title: Mixing bowl set volumes
text:
# Mixing bowl set volumes

## FAQ

**Can I use these bowls in the microwave?** Yes, all three are microwave-safe. The stoneware heats evenly, but the bowls themselves will get hot, so use a towel or oven mitt when removing them.

**Do the bowls have pouring spouts?** No. The rims are smooth and rounded without spouts. Tilt and pour slowly, or use a spatula to transfer thick batters.

**Will the largest bowl fit under a stand mixer?** Most stand mixers accommodate a 5-quart bowl, but clearance depends on your mixer model. Measure the distance from your mixer head to the base before planning to use the large bowl with a beater attachment.

title: Rug sizes and fit guide
text:
# Rug sizes and fit guide

The Wool Area Rug, 5x7 (FG-HOM-002) works in bedrooms, dining areas, and small living rooms. This guide shows how the 5×7 footprint fits common layouts so you can plan before it arrives.

title: Rug sizes and fit guide
text:
# Rug sizes and fit guide

## Bedroom placement

A 5×7 rug works as a runner at the foot of a queen or king bed. Position the 7-foot edge parallel to the bed frame, leaving 12 to 18 inches of exposed floor between the rug and the wall. The rug should extend 18 to 24 inches beyond each side of the mattress so your feet land on wool when you stand.

For twin or full beds, center the rug under the lower two-thirds of the bed frame with at least 18 inches extending past the foot. This creates a soft landing without overwhelming a smaller room.

title: Rug sizes and fit guide
text:
# Rug sizes and fit guide

## Living room configurations

In a small living room (10×12 feet or similar), center the rug under your main seating area. Front furniture legs—sofa, chairs, coffee table—should rest on the rug while back legs stay on the floor. This anchors the space without requiring a wall-to-wall fit.

If your room is narrower, float the rug entirely under the coffee table with all seating off the edges. Leave at least 8 inches of clearance between the rug perimeter and the nearest wall or baseboard.

title: Rug sizes and fit guide
text:
# Rug sizes and fit guide

## Dining room use

A 5×7 rug fits a small dining table (36 to 48 inches round or square) with four chairs. The rug should extend at least 24 inches beyond the table edge on all sides so chairs remain on the rug when pulled out. Measure your table diameter or width, add 48 inches, and confirm that total does not exceed 84 inches in either direction.

For rectangular tables longer than 60 inches, the 5×7 footprint will not provide adequate chair clearance. You will need a larger rug.

# Rug sizes and fit guide

## Care and material notes

The Wool Area Rug is hand-tufted, so expect slight size variation (within 2 inches) and a 1/2-inch pile height. New rugs may shed lightly for the first few weeks—vacuum on low suction without a beater bar. See our Wool rug cleaning guide for maintenance details and our Wool sourcing and certifications article for fiber specifications.

title: Rug sizes and fit guide
text:
# Rug sizes and fit guide

## FAQ

**Can I layer the 5×7 rug over wall-to-wall carpet?**  
Yes. Use a thin rug pad (not included) to prevent slipping. The combined pile height may create a trip edge, so ensure the rug lies flat.

**What if my space is between two layout examples?**  
Measure the furniture footprint, add your desired clearance (18 to 24 inches per side), and compare to 60×84 inches. If the numbers are close, the rug will work. If you need more than 6 extra inches in any direction, the fit will feel tight.

**Does the rug come with a pad?**  
No. Rug pads are sold separately and are not part of the Fieldstone catalog at this time.

# Linen throw blanket dimensions

The Linen Throw Blanket (FG-HOM-001) measures 50 inches by 70 inches. This is a standard throw size that works beautifully draped over furniture or as light coverage at the foot of a bed. The woven linen softens with each wash, so the blanket becomes more supple and inviting over time.

title: Linen throw blanket dimensions
text:
# Linen throw blanket dimensions

## Size and coverage

At 50 by 70 inches, the throw fits comfortably across a sofa or draped over an armchair. It covers about two-thirds of a queen or king bed when folded at the foot, offering an extra layer without overwhelming the bedding. The proportions suit one or two people for couch reading or watching a movie.

If you want full-body coverage, drape the throw lengthwise. At 70 inches, it extends from shoulders to feet for most adults. The 50-inch width wraps around one person or covers two people sitting close together. The blanket is not intended as a primary bedding layer—it functions as accent coverage or seasonal warmth.

title: Linen throw blanket dimensions
text:
# Linen throw blanket dimensions

## How linen behaves

Linen feels slightly textured and crisp when new. After the first wash, the fibers begin to relax. By the third or fourth wash cycle, the blanket develops a soft drape that conforms to your body or the furniture beneath it. This is normal and desirable. See our guide on linen washing instructions for care details that preserve the fabric's character.

The throw has a 1-inch hemmed edge on all sides. Linen can wrinkle easily, which is part of its appeal—the lived-in look suits casual spaces. If you prefer crisp edges, a warm iron on the linen setting smooths the fabric. Most customers leave it as-is.

title: Linen throw blanket dimensions
text:
# Linen throw blanket dimensions

## Comparing to other textiles

Cotton throws in this size range feel heavier and hold their shape more rigidly. Wool throws offer more insulation but less breathability. Linen balances weight and airflow, so it works across three seasons without feeling too warm or too light. The throw weighs about 1.8 pounds, light enough to toss in a tote bag for outdoor events.

For more on how we source and finish our linen, see about our linen. All Fieldstone linen products use European flax and are pre-washed once before shipment, so you will notice some softness immediately.

title: Linen throw blanket dimensions
text:
# Linen throw blanket dimensions

## FAQ

**Does the throw shrink after washing?** Expect up to 2% shrinkage in length and width after the first wash, so the dimensions may shift to approximately 49 by 68.5 inches. This is typical for linen and accounted for in the original cut.

**Can I use this as a picnic blanket?** Yes, though linen is more delicate than canvas or synthetic outdoor blankets. The throw works well on grass or sand for a few hours. Shake out debris and wash afterward to prevent staining.

**Will the throw pill or fray?** High-quality linen resists pilling. The hemmed edges are double-stitched to prevent fraying, but rough surfaces or pet claws can snag the weave. Trim any loose threads with scissors rather than pulling them.
